table.name: camunda_activity_instances
table.EngineParams: (timestamp) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(start_time) ORDER BY (process_definition_key, process_instance_id, start_time, id) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: camunda_incidents
table.EngineParams: (timestamp) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(create_time) ORDER BY (process_definition_key, create_time, id) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: camunda_process_instances
table.EngineParams: (timestamp) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(start_time) ORDER BY (process_definition_key, start_time, id) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: camunda_task_instances
table.EngineParams: (timestamp) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(start_time) ORDER BY (process_definition_key, process_instance_id, start_time, id) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: camunda_variable_updates
table.EngineParams: (timestamp) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(timestamp) ORDER BY (process_definition_key, process_instance_id, variable_name, timestamp) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: otel_logs
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(TimestampTime) PRIMARY KEY (ServiceName, TimestampTime) ORDER BY (ServiceName, TimestampTime, Timestamp) TTL TimestampTime + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: otel_metrics_exponential_histogram
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(TimeUnix) ORDER BY (ServiceName, MetricName, Attributes, toUnixTimestamp64Nano(TimeUnix)) TTL toDateTime(TimeUnix) + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: otel_metrics_gauge
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(TimeUnix) ORDER BY (ServiceName, MetricName, Attributes, toUnixTimestamp64Nano(TimeUnix)) TTL toDateTime(TimeUnix) + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: otel_metrics_histogram
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(TimeUnix) ORDER BY (ServiceName, MetricName, Attributes, toUnixTimestamp64Nano(TimeUnix)) TTL toDateTime(TimeUnix) + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: otel_metrics_sum
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(TimeUnix) ORDER BY (ServiceName, MetricName, Attributes, toUnixTimestamp64Nano(TimeUnix)) TTL toDateTime(TimeUnix) + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: otel_metrics_summary
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(TimeUnix) ORDER BY (ServiceName, MetricName, Attributes, toUnixTimestamp64Nano(TimeUnix)) TTL toDateTime(TimeUnix) + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: otel_traces
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(Timestamp) ORDER BY (ServiceName, SpanName, toDateTime(Timestamp)) TTL toDateTime(Timestamp) + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: otel_traces_trace_id_ts
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toDate(Start) ORDER BY (TraceId, Start) TTL toDateTime(Start) + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, ttl_only_drop_parts = 1

table.name: aggregate_function_combinators
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available aggregate function combinators, which could be applied to aggregate functions and change the way they work.

table.name: asynchronous_insert_log
table.Comment:
Contains a history for all asynchronous inserts executed on current server.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY event_date ORDER BY (database, `table`, event_date, event_time) TTL event_date + toIntervalDay(3) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: asynchronous_inserts
table.Comment: Contains information about pending asynchronous inserts in queue in server's memory.

table.name: asynchronous_loader
table.Comment: Contains information and status for recent asynchronous jobs (e.g. for tables loading). The table contains a row for every job.

table.name: asynchronous_metric_log
table.Comment:
Contains the historical values for system.asynchronous_metrics, once per time interval (one second by default).
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (metric, event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: asynchronous_metrics
table.Comment: Contains metrics that are calculated periodically in the background. For example, the amount of RAM in use.

table.name: azure_queue_metadata_cache
table.Comment: Contains in-memory state of AzureQueue metadata and currently processed rows per file.

table.name: azure_queue_settings
table.Comment: Contains a list of settings of AzureQueue tables.

table.name: background_schedule_pool
table.Comment: Contains information about tasks in all BackgroundSchedulePool instances. Each row represents a task.

table.name: background_schedule_pool_log
table.Comment:
Contains history of background schedule pool task executions.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: backups
table.Comment: Contains a list of all BACKUP or RESTORE operations with their current states and other properties. Note, that table is not persistent and it shows only operations executed after the last server restart.

table.name: build_options
table.Comment: Contains a list of all build flags, compiler options and commit hash for used build.

table.name: certificates
table.Comment: Contains information about available certificates and their sources.

table.name: clusters
table.Comment: Contains information about clusters defined in the configuration file or generated by a Replicated database.

table.name: codecs
table.Comment: Contains information about system codecs.

table.name: collations
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available collations for alphabetical comparison of strings.

table.name: columns
table.Comment: Lists all columns from all tables of the current server.

table.name: completions
table.Comment: Contains a list of completion tokens.

table.name: contributors
table.Comment: Contains a list of all ClickHouse contributors <3

table.name: current_roles
table.Comment: Contains active roles of a current user. SET ROLE changes the contents of this table.

table.name: dashboards
table.Comment: Contains queries used by /dashboard page accessible though HTTP interface. This table can be useful for monitoring and troubleshooting. The table contains a row for every chart in a dashboard.

table.name: data_skipping_indices
table.Comment: Contains all the information about all the data skipping indices in tables, similar to system.columns.

table.name: data_type_families
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available native data types along with all the aliases used for compatibility with other DBMS.

table.name: database_engines
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available database engines

table.name: database_replicas
table.Comment: Contains information and status of all database replicas on current server. Each database replica is represented by a single row.

table.name: databases
table.Comment: Lists all databases of the current server.

table.name: detached_parts
table.Comment: Contains a list of all parts which are being found in /detached directory along with a reason why it was detached. ClickHouse server doesn't use such parts anyhow.

table.name: detached_tables
table.Comment: Lists all detached tables of the current server.

table.name: dictionaries
table.Comment: Contains information about dictionaries.

table.name: dimensional_metrics
table.Comment: Contains dimensional metrics, which have multiple dimensions (labels) to provide more granular information. For example, counting failed merges by their error code. This table is always up to date.

table.name: disks
table.Comment: Contains information about disks defined in the server configuration.

table.name: distributed_ddl_queue
table.Comment: Contains information about distributed DDL queries (ON CLUSTER clause) that were executed on a cluster.

table.name: distribution_queue
table.Comment: Contains information about local files that are in the queue to be sent to the shards. These local files contain new parts that are created by inserting new data into the Distributed table in asynchronous mode.

table.name: dns_cache
table.Comment: Contains information about cached DNS records.

table.name: dropped_tables
table.Comment: Contains a list of tables which were dropped from Atomic databases but not completely removed yet.

table.name: dropped_tables_parts
table.Comment: Contains parts of system.dropped_tables tables

table.name: enabled_roles
table.Comment: Contains all active roles at the moment, including current role of the current user and granted roles for current role.

table.name: error_log
table.Comment:
Contains history of error values from table system.errors, periodically flushed to disk.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: errors
table.Comment: Contains a list of all errors which have ever happened including the error code, last time and message with unsymbolized stacktrace.

table.name: events
table.Comment: Contains profiling events and their current value.

table.name: fail_points
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available failpoints with their type and enabled status. Only available in debug builds.

table.name: filesystem_cache
table.Comment: Contains information about all entries inside filesystem cache for remote objects.

table.name: filesystem_cache_settings
table.Comment: Contains information about all filesystem cache settings

table.name: formats
table.Comment: Contains a list of all the formats along with flags whether a format is suitable for input/output or whether it supports parallelization.

table.name: functions
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available ordinary and aggregate functions with their descriptions.

table.name: grants
table.Comment: Contains the information about privileges granted to ClickHouse user accounts.

table.name: graphite_retentions
table.Comment: Contains information about parameters graphite_rollup which are used in tables with *GraphiteMergeTree engines.

table.name: histogram_metrics
table.Comment: Contains histogram metrics which can be calculated instantly and exported in the Prometheus format. For example, the keeper response time. This table is always up to date.

table.name: iceberg_history
table.Comment: Displays the history of an iceberg table similar to the Spark history table

table.name: instrumentation
table.Comment: Contains a list of all functions instrumented with XRay with their IDs and handlers.

table.name: jemalloc_bins
table.Comment: Contains information about memory allocations done via jemalloc allocator in different size classes (bins) aggregated from all arenas. These statistics might not be absolutely accurate because of thread local caching in jemalloc.

table.name: jemalloc_profile_text
table.Comment: Displays the symbolized jemalloc heap profile. Run 'SYSTEM JEMALLOC FLUSH PROFILE' to generate a profile first.

table.name: jemalloc_stats
table.Comment: Returns jemalloc statistics in a single row with a single column. Equivalent to SYSTEM JEMALLOC STATS command.

table.name: kafka_consumers
table.Comment: Contains information about Kafka consumers. Applicable for Kafka table engine (native ClickHouse integration).

table.name: keywords
table.Comment: Contains a list of all keywords used in ClickHouse parser.

table.name: licenses
table.Comment: Contains licenses of third-party libraries that are located in the contrib directory of ClickHouse sources.

table.name: macros
table.Comment: Contains a list of all macros defined in server configuration.

table.name: merge_tree_settings
table.Comment: Contains a list of all MergeTree engine specific settings, their current and default values along with descriptions. You may change any of them in SETTINGS section in CREATE query.

table.name: merges
table.Comment: Contains a list of merges currently executing merges of MergeTree tables and their progress. Each merge operation is represented by a single row.

table.name: metric_log
table.Comment:
Contains history of metrics values from tables system.metrics and system.events, periodically flushed to disk.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: metrics
table.Comment: Contains metrics which can be calculated instantly, or have a current value. For example, the number of simultaneously processed queries or the current replica delay. This table is always up to date.

table.name: models
table.Comment: Contains a list of CatBoost models loaded into a LibraryBridge's memory along with time when it was loaded.

table.name: moves
table.Comment: Contains information about in-progress data part moves of MergeTree tables. Each data part movement is represented by a single row.

table.name: mutations
table.Comment: Contains a list of mutations and their progress. Each mutation command is represented by a single row.

table.name: named_collections
table.Comment: Contains a list of all named collections which were created via SQL query or parsed from configuration file.

table.name: numbers
table.Comment: Generates all natural numbers, starting from 0 (to 2^64 - 1, and then again) in sorted order.

table.name: numbers_mt
table.Comment: Multithreaded version of `system.numbers`. Numbers order is not guaranteed.

table.name: one
table.Comment: This table contains a single row with a single dummy UInt8 column containing the value 0. Used when the table is not specified explicitly, for example in queries like `SELECT 1`.

table.name: part_log
table.Comment:
This table contains information about events that occurred with data parts in the MergeTree family tables, such as adding or merging data.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: part_moves_between_shards
table.Comment: Contains information about parts which are currently in a process of moving between shards and their progress.

table.name: parts
table.Comment: Contains a list of currently existing (both active and inactive) parts of all *-MergeTree tables. Each part is represented by a single row.

table.name: parts_columns
table.Comment: Contains a list of columns of all currently existing parts of all MergeTree tables. Each column is represented by a single row.

table.name: primes
table.Comment: Generates all prime numbers, starting from 2, in sorted order.

table.name: privileges
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available privileges that could be granted to a user or role.

table.name: processes
table.Comment: Contains a list of currently executing processes (queries) with their progress.

table.name: processors_profile_log
table.Comment:
Contains profiling information on processors level (building blocks for a pipeline for query execution.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) TTL event_date + toIntervalDay(30) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: projection_parts
table.Comment: Contains a list of currently existing projection parts (a copy of some part containing aggregated data or just sorted in different order) created for all the projections for all tables within a cluster.

table.name: projection_parts_columns
table.Comment: Contains a list of columns of all currently existing projection parts of all MergeTree tables. Each column is represented by a single row.

table.name: projections
table.Comment: Contains all the information about all the projections in tables, similar to system.data_skipping_indices.

table.name: query_cache
table.Comment: Contains information about all entries inside query cache in server's memory.

table.name: query_condition_cache
table.Comment: Contains information about all entries inside query condition cache in server's memory.

table.name: query_log
table.Comment:
Contains information about executed queries, for example, start time, duration of processing, error messages.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: quota_limits
table.Comment: Contains information about maximums for all intervals of all quotas. Any number of rows or zero can correspond to specific quota.

table.name: quota_usage
table.Comment: Contains quota usage by the current user: how much is used and how much is left.

table.name: quotas
table.Comment: Contains information about quotas.

table.name: quotas_usage
table.Comment: Contains quota usage by all users.

table.name: remote_data_paths
table.Comment: Contains a mapping from a filename on local filesystem to a blob name inside object storage.

table.name: replicas
table.Comment: Contains information and status of all table replicas on current server. Each replica is represented by a single row.

table.name: replicated_fetches
table.Comment: Contains information about currently running background fetches.

table.name: replicated_merge_tree_settings
table.Comment: Contains a list of all ReplicatedMergeTree engine specific settings, their current and default values along with descriptions. You may change any of them in SETTINGS section in CREATE query.

table.name: replication_queue
table.Comment: Contains information about tasks from replication queues stored in ClickHouse Keeper, or ZooKeeper, for each table replica.

table.name: resources
table.Comment: Contains a list of all currently existing resources.

table.name: rocksdb
table.Comment: Contains a list of metrics exposed from embedded RocksDB.

table.name: role_grants
table.Comment: Contains the role grants for users and roles. To add entries to this table, use `GRANT role TO user`. Using this table you may find out which roles are assigned to which users or which roles a user has.

table.name: roles
table.Comment: Contains a list of all roles created at the server.

table.name: row_policies
table.Comment: Contains filters for one particular table, as well as a list of roles and/or users which should use this row policy.

table.name: s3_queue_settings
table.Comment: Contains a list of settings of S3Queue tables.

table.name: s3queue_metadata_cache
table.Comment: Contains in-memory state of S3Queue metadata and currently processed rows per file.

table.name: scheduler
table.Comment: Contains information and status for scheduling nodes residing on the local server.

table.name: schema_inference_cache
table.Comment: Contains information about all cached file schemas.

table.name: server_settings
table.Comment: Contains a list of all server-wide settings (which are effective only on server startup and usually cannot be modified at runtime), their current and default values along with descriptions.

table.name: settings
table.Comment: Contains a list of all user-level settings (which can be modified in a scope of query or session), their current and default values along with descriptions.

table.name: settings_changes
table.Comment: Contains the information about the settings changes through different ClickHouse versions. You may make ClickHouse behave like a particular previous version by changing the `compatibility` user-level settings.

table.name: settings_profile_elements
table.Comment: Describes the content of each settings profile configured on the server. Including settings constraints, roles and users for which the settings are applied, and parent settings profiles.

table.name: settings_profiles
table.Comment: Contains properties of configured setting profiles.

table.name: stack_trace
table.Comment: Allows to obtain an unsymbolized stacktrace from all the threads of the server process.

table.name: storage_policies
table.Comment: Contains information about storage policies and volumes defined in the server configuration.

table.name: symbols
table.Comment: Contains information for introspection of ClickHouse binary. This table is only useful for C++ experts and ClickHouse engineers.

table.name: table_engines
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available table engines along with information whether a particular table engine supports some specific features (e.g. settings, skipping indices, projections, replication, TTL, deduplication, parallel insert, etc.)

table.name: table_functions
table.Comment: Contains a list of all available table functions with their descriptions.

table.name: tables
table.Comment: Lists all tables of the current server.

table.name: text_log
table.Comment:
Contains logging entries which are normally written to a log file or to stdout.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: time_zones
table.Comment: Contains a list of time zones that are supported by the ClickHouse server. This list of timezones might vary depending on the version of ClickHouse.

table.name: tokenizers
table.Comment: Contains a list of the available tokenizers.

table.name: trace_log
table.Comment:
Contains stack traces collected by the sampling query profiler.
It is safe to truncate or drop this table at any time.
table.EngineParams: PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_date) ORDER BY (event_date, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192

table.name: unicode
table.Comment: Contains all unicode codepoints.

table.name: user_defined_functions
table.Comment: Shows all user-defined executable functions with their metadata, configuration, and loading status.

table.name: user_directories
table.Comment: Contains the information about configured user directories - directories on the file system from which ClickHouse server is allowed to read user provided data.

table.name: user_processes
table.Comment: This system table can be used to get overview of memory usage and ProfileEvents of users.

table.name: users
table.Comment: Contains a list of all users profiles either configured at the server through a configuration file or created via SQL.

table.name: view_refreshes
table.Comment: Lists all Refreshable Materialized Views of current server.

table.name: warnings
table.Comment: Contains warnings about server configuration to be displayed by clickhouse-client right after it connects to the server.

table.name: workloads
table.Comment: Contains a list of all currently existing workloads.

table.name: zeros
table.Comment: Produces unlimited number of non-materialized zeros.

table.name: zeros_mt
table.Comment: Multithreaded version of system.zeros.

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of the combinator.

column.name: is_internal
column.Comment: Whether this combinator is for internal usage only.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: The date when the async insert happened.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: The date and time when the async insert finished execution.

column.name: event_time_microseconds
column.Comment: The date and time when the async insert finished execution with microseconds precision.

column.name: query
column.Comment: Query string.

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the database the table is in.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: format
column.Comment: Format name.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: ID of the initial query.

column.name: bytes
column.Comment: Number of inserted bytes.

column.name: rows
column.Comment: Number of inserted rows.

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Exception message.

column.name: status
column.Comment: Status of the view. Values: 'Ok' = 1 — Successful insert, 'ParsingError' = 2 — Exception when parsing the data, 'FlushError' = 3 — Exception when flushing the data

column.name: data_kind
column.Comment: The status of the data. Value: 'Parsed' and 'Preprocessed'.

column.name: flush_time
column.Comment: The date and time when the flush happened.

column.name: flush_time_microseconds
column.Comment: The date and time when the flush happened with microseconds precision.

column.name: flush_query_id
column.Comment: ID of the flush query.

column.name: timeout_milliseconds
column.Comment: The adaptive timeout calculated for this entry.

column.name: query
column.Comment: Query text.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: format
column.Comment: Format name.

column.name: first_update
column.Comment: First insert time with microseconds resolution.

column.name: total_bytes
column.Comment: Total number of bytes waiting in the queue.

column.name: entries.query_id
column.Comment: Array of query ids of the inserts waiting in the queue.

column.name: entries.bytes
column.Comment: Array of bytes of each insert query waiting in the queue.

column.name: job
column.Comment: Job name (may be not unique).

column.name: job_id
column.Comment: Unique ID of the job.

column.name: dependencies
column.Comment: List of IDs of jobs that should be done before this job.

column.name: dependencies_left
column.Comment: Current number of dependencies left to be done.

column.name: status
column.Comment: Current load status of a job: PENDING: Load job is not started yet. OK: Load job executed and was successful. FAILED: Load job executed and failed. CANCELED: Load job is not going to be executed due to removal or dependency failure.

column.name: is_executing
column.Comment: The job is currently being executed by a worker.

column.name: is_blocked
column.Comment: The job waits for its dependencies to be done.

column.name: is_ready
column.Comment: The job is ready to be executed and waits for a worker.

column.name: elapsed
column.Comment: Seconds elapsed since start of execution. Zero if job is not started. Total execution time if job finished.

column.name: pool_id
column.Comment: ID of a pool currently assigned to the job.

column.name: pool
column.Comment: Name of `pool_id` pool.

column.name: priority
column.Comment: Priority of `pool_id` pool.

column.name: execution_pool_id
column.Comment: ID of a pool the job is executed in. Equals initially assigned pool before execution starts.

column.name: execution_pool
column.Comment: Name of `execution_pool_id` pool.

column.name: execution_priority
column.Comment: Priority of execution_pool_id pool.

column.name: ready_seqno
column.Comment: Not null for ready jobs. Worker pulls the next job to be executed from a ready queue of its pool. If there are multiple ready jobs, then job with the lowest value of `ready_seqno` is picked.

column.name: waiters
column.Comment: The number of threads waiting on this job.

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Not null for failed and canceled jobs. Holds error message raised during query execution or error leading to cancelling of this job along with dependency failure chain of job names.

column.name: schedule_time
column.Comment: Time when job was created and scheduled to be executed (usually with all its dependencies).

column.name: enqueue_time
column.Comment: Time when job became ready and was enqueued into a ready queue of it's pool. Null if the job is not ready yet.

column.name: start_time
column.Comment: Time when worker dequeues the job from ready queue and start its execution. Null if the job is not started yet.

column.name: finish_time
column.Comment: Time when job execution is finished. Null if the job is not finished yet.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: Event date.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: Event time.

column.name: metric
column.Comment: Metric name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Metric value.

column.name: metric
column.Comment: Metric name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Metric value.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Metric description.

column.name: zookeeper_path
column.Comment: Path in zookeeper to metadata

column.name: file_path
column.Comment: File path of a file which is being processed

column.name: file_name
column.Comment: File name of a file which is being processed

column.name: rows_processed
column.Comment: Currently processed number of rows

column.name: status
column.Comment: Status of processing: Processed, Processing, Failed

column.name: processing_start_time
column.Comment: Time at which processing of the file started

column.name: processing_end_time
column.Comment: Time at which processing of the file ended

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Exception which happened during processing

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database of the table with S3Queue Engine.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table with S3Queue Engine.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Setting name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Setting value.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Setting type (implementation specific string value).

column.name: changed
column.Comment: 1 if the setting was explicitly defined in the config or explicitly changed.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Setting description.

column.name: alterable
column.Comment: Shows whether the current user can change the setting via ALTER TABLE MODIFY SETTING: 0 — Current user can change the setting, 1 — Current user can't change the setting.

column.name: pool
column.Comment: Pool name.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: table_uuid
column.Comment: Table UUID.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: Query ID (if executing now).

column.name: elapsed_ms
column.Comment: Task execution time (if executing now).

column.name: log_name
column.Comment: Log name for the task.

column.name: deactivated
column.Comment: Whether the task is deactivated (always false, since deactivated tasks removed from the pool).

column.name: scheduled
column.Comment: Whether the task is scheduled for execution.

column.name: delayed
column.Comment: Whether the task is scheduled with delay.

column.name: executing
column.Comment: Whether the task is currently executing.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: Event date.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: Event time.

column.name: event_time_microseconds
column.Comment: Event time with microseconds precision.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: Identifier of the query associated with the background task.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: table_uuid
column.Comment: UUID of the table the background task belongs to.

column.name: log_name
column.Comment: Name of the background task.

column.name: duration_ms
column.Comment: Duration of the task execution in milliseconds.

column.name: error
column.Comment: The error code of the occurred exception.

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Text message of the occurred error.

column.name: id
column.Comment: Operation ID, can be either passed via SETTINGS id=... or be randomly generated UUID.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Operation name, a string like `Disk('backups', 'my_backup')`

column.name: base_backup_name
column.Comment: Base Backup Operation name, a string like `Disk('backups', 'my_base_backup')`

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: Query ID of a query that started backup.

column.name: status
column.Comment: Status of backup or restore operation.

column.name: error
column.Comment: The error message if any.

column.name: start_time
column.Comment: The time when operation started.

column.name: end_time
column.Comment: The time when operation finished.

column.name: num_files
column.Comment: The number of files stored in the backup.

column.name: total_size
column.Comment: The total size of files stored in the backup.

column.name: num_entries
column.Comment: The number of entries in the backup, i.e. the number of files inside the folder if the backup is stored as a folder.

column.name: uncompressed_size
column.Comment: The uncompressed size of the backup.

column.name: compressed_size
column.Comment: The compressed size of the backup.

column.name: files_read
column.Comment: Returns the number of files read during RESTORE from this backup.

column.name: bytes_read
column.Comment: Returns the total size of files read during RESTORE from this backup.

column.name: ProfileEvents
column.Comment: All the profile events captured during this operation.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the build option.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Value of the build option.

column.name: version
column.Comment: Version of the certificate. Values are 0 for v1, 1 for v2, 2 for v3.

column.name: serial_number
column.Comment: Serial Number of the certificate assigned by the issuer.

column.name: signature_algo
column.Comment: Signature Algorithm - an algorithm used by the issuer to sign this certificate.

column.name: issuer
column.Comment: Issuer - an unique identifier for the Certificate Authority issuing this certificate.

column.name: not_before
column.Comment: The beginning of the time window when this certificate is valid.

column.name: not_after
column.Comment: The end of the time window when this certificate is valid.

column.name: subject
column.Comment: Subject - identifies the owner of the public key.

column.name: pkey_algo
column.Comment: Public Key Algorithm defines the algorithm the public key can be used with.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Path to the file or directory containing this certificate.

column.name: default
column.Comment: Certificate is in the default certificate location.

column.name: protocol
column.Comment: Protocol name if certificate is from per-protocol TLS config, otherwise empty.

column.name: cluster
column.Comment: The cluster name.

column.name: shard_num
column.Comment: The shard number in the cluster, starting from 1.

column.name: shard_name
column.Comment: The name of the shard in the cluster.

column.name: shard_weight
column.Comment: The relative weight of the shard when writing data.

column.name: internal_replication
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether this host is a part on ensemble which can replicate the data on its own.

column.name: replica_num
column.Comment: The replica number in the shard, starting from 1.

column.name: host_name
column.Comment: The host name, as specified in the config.

column.name: host_address
column.Comment: The host IP address obtained from DNS.

column.name: port
column.Comment: The port to use for connecting to the server.

column.name: is_local
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the host is local.

column.name: user
column.Comment: The name of the user for connecting to the server.

column.name: default_database
column.Comment: The default database name.

column.name: errors_count
column.Comment: The number of times this host failed to reach replica.

column.name: slowdowns_count
column.Comment: The number of slowdowns that led to changing replica when establishing a connection with hedged requests.

column.name: estimated_recovery_time
column.Comment: Seconds remaining until the replica error count is zeroed and it is considered to be back to normal.

column.name: database_shard_name
column.Comment: The name of the `Replicated` database shard (for clusters that belong to a `Replicated` database).

column.name: database_replica_name
column.Comment: The name of the `Replicated` database replica (for clusters that belong to a `Replicated` database).

column.name: is_shared_catalog_cluster
column.Comment: Bool indicating if the cluster belongs to shared catalog.

column.name: is_active
column.Comment: The status of the Replicated database replica (for clusters that belong to a Replicated database): 1 means 'replica is online', 0 means 'replica is offline', NULL means 'unknown'.

column.name: unsynced_after_recovery
column.Comment: Indicates if a Replicated database replica has replication lag more than max_replication_lag_to_enqueue after creating or recovering the replica.

column.name: replication_lag
column.Comment: The replication lag of the `Replicated` database replica (for clusters that belong to a Replicated database).

column.name: recovery_time
column.Comment: The recovery time of the `Replicated` database replica (for clusters that belong to a Replicated database), in milliseconds.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Codec name.

column.name: method_byte
column.Comment: Byte which indicates codec in compressed file.

column.name: is_compression
column.Comment: True if this codec compresses something. Otherwise it can be just a transformation that helps compression.

column.name: is_generic_compression
column.Comment: The codec is a generic compression algorithm like lz4, zstd.

column.name: is_encryption
column.Comment: The codec encrypts.

column.name: is_timeseries_codec
column.Comment: The codec is for floating point timeseries codec.

column.name: is_experimental
column.Comment: The codec is experimental.

column.name: description
column.Comment: A high-level description of the codec.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the collation.

column.name: language
column.Comment: The language.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Column name.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Column type.

column.name: position
column.Comment: Ordinal position of a column in a table starting with 1.

column.name: default_kind
column.Comment: Expression type (DEFAULT, MATERIALIZED, ALIAS) for the default value, or an empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: default_expression
column.Comment: Expression for the default value, or an empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: The size of compressed data, in bytes.

column.name: data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: The size of decompressed data, in bytes.

column.name: marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of marks, in bytes.

column.name: comment
column.Comment: Comment on the column, or an empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: is_in_partition_key
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the column is in the partition expression.

column.name: is_in_sorting_key
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the column is in the sorting key expression.

column.name: is_in_primary_key
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the column is in the primary key expression.

column.name: is_in_sampling_key
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the column is in the sampling key expression.

column.name: compression_codec
column.Comment: Compression codec name.

column.name: character_octet_length
column.Comment: Maximum length in bytes for binary data, character data, or text data and images. In ClickHouse makes sense only for FixedString data type. Otherwise, the NULL value is returned.

column.name: numeric_precision
column.Comment: Accuracy of approximate numeric data, exact numeric data, integer data, or monetary data. In ClickHouse it is bit width for integer types and decimal precision for Decimal types. Otherwise, the NULL value is returned.

column.name: numeric_precision_radix
column.Comment: The base of the number system is the accuracy of approximate numeric data, exact numeric data, integer data or monetary data. In ClickHouse it's 2 for integer types and 10 for Decimal types. Otherwise, the NULL value is returned.

column.name: numeric_scale
column.Comment: The scale of approximate numeric data, exact numeric data, integer data, or monetary data. In ClickHouse makes sense only for Decimal types. Otherwise, the NULL value is returned.

column.name: datetime_precision
column.Comment: Decimal precision of DateTime64 data type. For other data types, the NULL value is returned.

column.name: serialization_hint
column.Comment: A hint for column to choose serialization on inserts according to statistics.

column.name: statistics
column.Comment: The types of statistics created in this columns.

column.name: word
column.Comment: Completion token.

column.name: context
column.Comment: Token entity kind (e.g. table).

column.name: belongs
column.Comment: Token for entity, this token belongs to (e.g. name of owning database).

column.name: name
column.Comment: Contributor (author) name from git log.

column.name: role_name
column.Comment: Role name.

column.name: with_admin_option
column.Comment: 1 if the role has ADMIN OPTION privilege.

column.name: is_default
column.Comment: 1 if role is set to be a default.

column.name: dashboard
column.Comment: The dashboard name.

column.name: title
column.Comment: The title of a chart.

column.name: query
column.Comment: The query to obtain data to be displayed.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Index name.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Index type.

column.name: type_full
column.Comment: Index type expression from create statement.

column.name: expr
column.Comment: Expression for the index calculation.

column.name: creation
column.Comment: Whether the index was created implicitly (via add_minmax_index_for_numeric_columns or similar)

column.name: granularity
column.Comment: The number of granules in the block.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: The size of compressed data, in bytes.

column.name: data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: The size of decompressed data, in bytes.

column.name: marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of marks, in bytes.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Data type name.

column.name: case_insensitive
column.Comment: Property that shows whether you can use a data type name in a query in case insensitive manner or not. For example, `Date` and `date` are both valid.

column.name: alias_to
column.Comment: Data type name for which `name` is an alias.

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of database engine.

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the Replicated database is in.

column.name: is_readonly
column.Comment: Whether the database replica is in read-only mode.

column.name: max_log_ptr
column.Comment: Maximum entry number in the log of general activity.

column.name: replica_name
column.Comment: Replica name in ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: replica_path
column.Comment: Path to replica data in ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: zookeeper_path
column.Comment: Path to database data in ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: shard_name
column.Comment: The name of the shard in the cluster.

column.name: log_ptr
column.Comment: Maximum entry number in the log of general activity that the replica copied to its execution queue, plus one.

column.name: total_replicas
column.Comment: The total number of known replicas of this database.

column.name: zookeeper_exception
column.Comment: The last exception message, got if the error happened when fetching the info from ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: is_session_expired
column.Comment: The session with ClickHouse Keeper has expired. Basically the same as `is_readonly`.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Database engine.

column.name: data_path
column.Comment: Data path.

column.name: metadata_path
column.Comment: Metadata path.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: Database UUID.

column.name: engine_full
column.Comment: Parameters of the database engine.

column.name: comment
column.Comment: Database comment.

column.name: is_external
column.Comment: Database is external (i.e. PostgreSQL/DataLakeCatalog).

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the database this part belongs to.

column.name: table
column.Comment: The name of the table this part belongs to.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: The identifier of the partition this part belongs to.

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of the part.

column.name: bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the data part files in bytes.

column.name: modification_time
column.Comment: The time the directory with the data part was modified. This usually corresponds to the time when detach happened.

column.name: disk
column.Comment: The name of the disk that stores this data part.

column.name: path
column.Comment: The path of the disk to the file of this data part.

column.name: reason
column.Comment: The explanation why this part was detached.

column.name: min_block_number
column.Comment: The minimum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: max_block_number
column.Comment: The maximum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Depth of the merge tree. Zero means that the current part was created by insert rather than by merging other parts.

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the database the table is in.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: Table uuid (Atomic database).

column.name: metadata_path
column.Comment: Path to the table metadata in the file system.

column.name: is_permanently
column.Comment: Table was detached permanently.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database containing the dictionary created by DDL query. Empty string for other dictionaries.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Dictionary name.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: Dictionary UUID.

column.name: status
column.Comment: Dictionary status. Possible values: NOT_LOADED — Dictionary was not loaded because it was not used, LOADED — Dictionary loaded successfully, FAILED — Unable to load the dictionary as a result of an error, LOADING — Dictionary is loading now, LOADED_AND_RELOADING — Dictionary is loaded successfully, and is being reloaded right now (frequent reasons: SYSTEM RELOAD DICTIONARY query, timeout, dictionary config has changed), FAILED_AND_RELOADING — Could not load the dictionary as a result of an error and is loading now.

column.name: origin
column.Comment: Path to the configuration file that describes the dictionary.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Type of a dictionary allocation. Storing Dictionaries in Memory.

column.name: key.names
column.Comment: Array of key names provided by the dictionary.

column.name: key.types
column.Comment: Corresponding array of key types provided by the dictionary.

column.name: attribute.names
column.Comment: Array of attribute names provided by the dictionary.

column.name: attribute.types
column.Comment: Corresponding array of attribute types provided by the dictionary.

column.name: bytes_allocated
column.Comment: Amount of RAM allocated for the dictionary.

column.name: hierarchical_index_bytes_allocated
column.Comment: Amount of RAM allocated for hierarchical index.

column.name: query_count
column.Comment: Number of queries since the dictionary was loaded or since the last successful reboot.

column.name: hit_rate
column.Comment: For cache dictionaries, the percentage of uses for which the value was in the cache.

column.name: found_rate
column.Comment: The percentage of uses for which the value was found.

column.name: element_count
column.Comment: Number of items stored in the dictionary.

column.name: load_factor
column.Comment: Percentage filled in the dictionary (for a hashed dictionary, the percentage filled in the hash table).

column.name: source
column.Comment: Text describing the data source for the dictionary.

column.name: lifetime_min
column.Comment: Minimum lifetime of the dictionary in memory, after which ClickHouse tries to reload the dictionary (if invalidate_query is set, then only if it has changed). Set in seconds.

column.name: lifetime_max
column.Comment: Maximum lifetime of the dictionary in memory, after which ClickHouse tries to reload the dictionary (if invalidate_query is set, then only if it has changed). Set in seconds.

column.name: loading_start_time
column.Comment: Start time for loading the dictionary.

column.name: last_successful_update_time
column.Comment: End time for loading or updating the dictionary. Helps to monitor some troubles with dictionary sources and investigate the causes.

column.name: error_count
column.Comment: Number of errors since last successful loading. Helps to monitor some troubles with dictionary sources and investigate the causes.

column.name: loading_duration
column.Comment: Duration of a dictionary loading.

column.name: last_exception
column.Comment: Text of the error that occurs when creating or reloading the dictionary if the dictionary couldn't be created.

column.name: comment
column.Comment: Text of the comment to dictionary.

column.name: metric
column.Comment: Metric name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Metric value.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Metric description.

column.name: labels
column.Comment: Metric labels.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of a disk in the server configuration.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Path to the mount point in the file system.

column.name: free_space
column.Comment: Free space on disk in bytes.

column.name: total_space
column.Comment: Disk volume in bytes.

column.name: unreserved_space
column.Comment: Free space which is not taken by reservations (free_space minus the size of reservations taken by merges, inserts, and other disk write operations currently running).

column.name: keep_free_space
column.Comment: Amount of disk space that should stay free on disk in bytes. Defined in the keep_free_space_bytes parameter of disk configuration.

column.name: type
column.Comment: The disk type which tells where this disk stores the data - RAM, local drive or remote storage.

column.name: object_storage_type
column.Comment: Type of object storage if disk type is object_storage

column.name: metadata_type
column.Comment: Type of metadata storage if disk type is object_storage

column.name: is_encrypted
column.Comment: Flag which shows whether this disk ecrypts the underlying data.

column.name: is_read_only
column.Comment: Flag which indicates that you can only perform read operations with this disk.

column.name: is_write_once
column.Comment: Flag which indicates if disk is write-once. Which means that it does support BACKUP to this disk, but does not support INSERT into MergeTree table on this disk.

column.name: is_remote
column.Comment: Flag which indicated what operations with this disk involve network interaction.

column.name: is_broken
column.Comment: Flag which indicates if disk is broken. Broken disks will have 0 space and cannot be used.

column.name: cache_path
column.Comment: The path to the cache directory on local drive in case when the disk supports caching.

column.name: entry
column.Comment: Query id.

column.name: entry_version
column.Comment: Version of the entry.

column.name: initiator_host
column.Comment: Host that initiated the DDL operation.

column.name: initiator_port
column.Comment: Port used by the initiator.

column.name: cluster
column.Comment: Cluster name, empty if not determined.

column.name: query
column.Comment: Query executed.

column.name: settings
column.Comment: Settings used in the DDL operation.

column.name: query_create_time
column.Comment: Query created time.

column.name: host
column.Comment: Hostname.

column.name: port
column.Comment: Host Port.

column.name: status
column.Comment: Status of the query.

column.name: exception_code
column.Comment: Exception code.

column.name: exception_text
column.Comment: Exception message.

column.name: query_finish_time
column.Comment: Query finish time.

column.name: query_duration_ms
column.Comment: Duration of query execution (in milliseconds).

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: data_path
column.Comment: Path to the folder with local files.

column.name: is_blocked
column.Comment: Flag indicates whether sending local files to the server is blocked.

column.name: error_count
column.Comment: Number of errors.

column.name: data_files
column.Comment: Number of local files in a folder.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Size of compressed data in local files, in bytes.

column.name: broken_data_files
column.Comment: Number of files that has been marked as broken (due to an error).

column.name: broken_data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Size of compressed data in broken files, in bytes.

column.name: last_exception
column.Comment: Text message about the last error that occurred (if any).

column.name: last_exception_time
column.Comment: Time when last exception occurred.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname.

column.name: ip_address
column.Comment: IP address.

column.name: ip_family
column.Comment: IP address family.

column.name: cached_at
column.Comment: Record cached timestamp.

column.name: index
column.Comment: Index in marked_dropped_tables queue.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: Table UUID.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Table engine name.

column.name: metadata_dropped_path
column.Comment: Path of table's metadata file in metadata_dropped directory.

column.name: table_dropped_time
column.Comment: The time when the next attempt to remove table's data is scheduled on. Usually it's the table when the table was dropped plus `database_atomic_delay_before_drop_table_sec`.

column.name: partition
column.Comment: The partition name.

column.name: name
column.Comment:
Name of the data part. The part naming structure can be used to determine many aspects of the data, ingest, and merge patterns. The part naming format is the following:
``` <partition_id>_<minimum_block_number>_<maximum_block_number>_<level>_<data_version> ```
* Definitions: - `partition_id` - identifies the partition key - `minimum_block_number` - identifies the minimum block number in the part. ClickHouse always merges continuous blocks - `maximum_block_number` - identifies the maximum block number in the part - `level` - incremented by one with each additional merge on the part. A level of 0 indicates this is a new part that has not been merged. It is important to remember that all parts in ClickHouse are always immutable - `data_version` - optional value, incremented when a part is mutated (again, mutated data is always only written to a new part, since parts are immutable)

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: The UUID of data part.

column.name: part_type
column.Comment: The data part storing format. Possible Values: Wide (a file per column) and Compact (a single file for all columns).

column.name: active
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the data part is active. If a data part is active, it's used in a table. Otherwise, it's about to be deleted. Inactive data parts appear after merging and mutating operations.

column.name: marks
column.Comment: The number of marks. To get the approximate number of rows in a data part, multiply marks by the index granularity (usually 8192) (this hint does not work for adaptive granularity).

column.name: rows
column.Comment: The number of rows.

column.name: files
column.Comment: The number of files in the data part.

column.name: bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the data part files in bytes.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: primary_key_size
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by primary key values in the primary.idx/cidx file on disk.

column.name: marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks.

column.name: secondary_indices_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data for secondary indices in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: secondary_indices_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data for secondary indices in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: secondary_indices_marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks for secondary indices.

column.name: modification_time
column.Comment: The time the directory with the data part was modified. This usually corresponds to the time of data part creation.

column.name: remove_time
column.Comment: The time when the data part became inactive.

column.name: refcount
column.Comment: The number of places where the data part is used. A value greater than 2 indicates that the data part is used in queries or merges.

column.name: min_date
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date key in the data part.

column.name: max_date
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date key in the data part.

column.name: min_time
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date and time key in the data part.

column.name: max_time
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date and time key in the data part.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: ID of the partition.

column.name: min_block_number
column.Comment: The minimum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: max_block_number
column.Comment: The maximum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Depth of the merge tree. Zero means that the current part was created by insert rather than by merging other parts.

column.name: data_version
column.Comment: Number that is used to determine which mutations should be applied to the data part (mutations with a version higher than data_version).

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by primary key values.

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory_allocated
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) reserved for primary key values.

column.name: index_granularity_bytes_in_memory
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by index granularity values (will be 0 in case of primary_key_lazy_load=1 and use_primary_key_cache=1).

column.name: index_granularity_bytes_in_memory_allocated
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) reserved for index granularity values (will be 0 in case of primary_key_lazy_load=1 and use_primary_key_cache=1).

column.name: is_frozen
column.Comment: Flag that shows that a partition data backup exists. 1, the backup exists. 0, the backup does not exist.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Name of the table engine without parameters.

column.name: disk_name
column.Comment: Name of a disk that stores the data part.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Absolute path to the folder with data part files.

column.name: hash_of_all_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of compressed files.

column.name: hash_of_uncompressed_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of uncompressed files (files with marks, index file etc.).

column.name: uncompressed_hash_of_compressed_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of data in the compressed files as if they were uncompressed.

column.name: delete_ttl_info_min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date and time key for TTL DELETE rule.

column.name: delete_ttl_info_max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date and time key for TTL DELETE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: Array of expressions. Each expression defines a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: Array of date and time values. Each element describes the minimum key value for a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: Array of date and time values. Each element describes the maximum key value for a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: default_compression_codec
column.Comment: The name of the codec used to compress this data part (in case when there is no explicit codec for columns).

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: projections
column.Comment: The list of projection names calculated for this part.

column.name: visible
column.Comment: Flag which indicated whether this part is visible for SELECT queries.

column.name: creation_tid
column.Comment: ID of transaction that has created/is trying to create this object.

column.name: removal_tid_lock
column.Comment: Hash of removal_tid, used to lock an object for removal.

column.name: removal_tid
column.Comment: ID of transaction that has removed/is trying to remove this object

column.name: creation_csn
column.Comment: CSN of transaction that has created this object

column.name: removal_csn
column.Comment: CSN of transaction that has removed this object

column.name: has_lightweight_delete
column.Comment: The flag which indicated whether the part has lightweight delete mask.

column.name: last_removal_attempt_time
column.Comment: The last time the server tried to delete this part.

column.name: removal_state
column.Comment: The current state of part removal process.

column.name: role_name
column.Comment: Role name.

column.name: with_admin_option
column.Comment: 1 if the role has ADMIN OPTION privilege.

column.name: is_current
column.Comment: Flag that shows whether `enabled_role` is a current role of a current user.

column.name: is_default
column.Comment: Flag that shows whether `enabled_role` is a default role.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: Event date.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: Event time.

column.name: code
column.Comment: Error code.

column.name: error
column.Comment: Error name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Number of errors happened in time interval.

column.name: remote
column.Comment: Remote exception (i.e. received during one of the distributed queries).

column.name: last_error_time
column.Comment: The time when the last error happened.

column.name: last_error_message
column.Comment: Message for the last error.

column.name: last_error_query_id
column.Comment: Id of a query that caused the last error (if available).

column.name: last_error_trace
column.Comment: A stack trace that represents a list of physical addresses where the called methods are stored.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the error (errorCodeToName).

column.name: code
column.Comment: Code number of the error.

column.name: value
column.Comment: The number of times this error happened.

column.name: last_error_time
column.Comment: The time when the last error happened.

column.name: last_error_message
column.Comment: Message for the last error.

column.name: last_error_format_string
column.Comment: Format string for the last error.

column.name: last_error_trace
column.Comment: A stack trace that represents a list of physical addresses where the called methods are stored.

column.name: remote
column.Comment: Remote exception (i.e. received during one of the distributed queries).

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: Id of a query that caused an error (if available).

column.name: event
column.Comment: Event name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Number of events occurred.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Event description.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the failpoint.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Type of failpoint: 'once' fires a single time then auto-disables, 'regular' fires every time, 'pauseable_once' blocks execution once, 'pauseable' blocks execution every time until resumed.

column.name: enabled
column.Comment: Whether the failpoint is currently enabled (1) or disabled (0).

column.name: cache_name
column.Comment: Name of the cache object. Can be used in `SYSTEM DESCRIBE FILESYSTEM CACHE <name>`, `SYSTEM DROP FILESYSTEM CACHE <name>` commands

column.name: cache_base_path
column.Comment: Path to the base directory where all caches files (of a cache identidied by `cache_name`) are stored.

column.name: cache_path
column.Comment: Path to a particular cache file, corresponding to a file segment in a source file

column.name: key
column.Comment: Cache key of the file segment

column.name: file_segment_range_begin
column.Comment: Offset corresponding to the beginning of the file segment range

column.name: file_segment_range_end
column.Comment: Offset corresponding to the (including) end of the file segment range

column.name: size
column.Comment: Size of the file segment

column.name: state
column.Comment: File segment state (DOWNLOADED, DOWNLOADING, PARTIALLY_DOWNLOADED, ...)

column.name: finished_download_time
column.Comment: Time when file segment finished downloading.

column.name: cache_hits
column.Comment: Number of cache hits of corresponding file segment

column.name: references
column.Comment: Number of references to corresponding file segment. Value 1 means that nobody uses it at the moment (the only existing reference is in cache storage itself)

column.name: downloaded_size
column.Comment: Downloaded size of the file segment

column.name: kind
column.Comment: File segment kind (used to distringuish between file segments added as a part of 'Temporary data in cache')

column.name: unbound
column.Comment: Internal implementation flag

column.name: user_id
column.Comment: User id of the user which created the file segment

column.name: segment_type
column.Comment: Type of the segment. Used to separate data files(`.json`, `.txt` and etc) from data file(`.bin`, mark files).

column.name: file_size
column.Comment: File size of the file to which current file segment belongs

column.name: cache_name
column.Comment: Cache name

column.name: path
column.Comment: Cache directory path

column.name: max_size
column.Comment: Maximum cache size

column.name: max_elements
column.Comment: Maximum number of cache elements, e.g. file segments (limits number of files on filesystem)

column.name: max_file_segment_size
column.Comment: Maximum size of a single file segment

column.name: boundary_alignment
column.Comment: File segment alignment

column.name: cache_on_write_operations
column.Comment: Enables write-through cache (cache on INSERT and MERGE)

column.name: cache_policy
column.Comment: Cache eviction policy

column.name: slru_size_ratio
column.Comment: SLRU cache policy size ratio of protected to probationary elements

column.name: background_download_threads
column.Comment: Number of background download threads. Value 0 disables background download

column.name: background_download_queue_size_limit
column.Comment: Size of background download queue. Value 0 disables background download

column.name: background_download_max_file_segment_size
column.Comment: Maximum size which can be downloaded in background download

column.name: load_metadata_threads
column.Comment: Number of threads to load cache metadata at server startup. Value 0 disables asynchronous loading of metadata

column.name: load_metadata_asynchronously
column.Comment: Enables asynchronous loading of metadata on server startup

column.name: keep_free_space_size_ratio
column.Comment: A ratio of free space which cache would try to uphold in the background

column.name: keep_free_space_elements_ratio
column.Comment: A ratio of free elements which cache would try to uphold in the background

column.name: keep_free_space_remove_batch
column.Comment: A remove batch size of cache elements made by background thread which upholds free space/elements ratio

column.name: enable_filesystem_query_cache_limit
column.Comment: Enable limiting maximum size of cache which can be written within a query

column.name: cache_hits_threshold
column.Comment: Deprecated setting

column.name: enable_bypass_cache_with_threshold
column.Comment: Undocumented. Not recommended for use

column.name: bypass_cache_threshold
column.Comment: Undocumented. Not recommended for use

column.name: write_cache_per_user_id_directory
column.Comment: Internal ClickHouse Cloud setting

column.name: allow_dynamic_cache_resize
column.Comment: Allow dynamic resize of filesystem cache

column.name: max_size_ratio_to_total_space
column.Comment: Ratio of `max_size` to total disk space

column.name: use_split_cache
column.Comment: Use separation of files to system/data.

column.name: split_cache_ratio
column.Comment: Ratio of system segment to total size of cache for split_cache.

column.name: overcommit_eviction_evict_step
column.Comment: Eviction step in bytes for overcommit eviction policy. Used for keep_free_space_*_ratio settings

column.name: check_cache_probability
column.Comment: Works only for debug or sanitizer build. Checks cache correctness by going through all cache and checking state of each cache element

column.name: is_initialized
column.Comment: Indicates whether cache was successfully initialized

column.name: current_size
column.Comment: Current cache size

column.name: current_elements_num
column.Comment: Current cache elements (file segments) number

column.name: name
column.Comment: Format name.

column.name: is_input
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the format is suitable for data input.

column.name: is_output
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the format is suitable for data output.

column.name: supports_parallel_parsing
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the format supports parallel parsing.

column.name: supports_parallel_formatting
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the format supports parallel formatting.

column.name: is_tty_friendly
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the format usually displays fine in the terminal. For other formats, CLI will ask before output.

column.name: content_type
column.Comment: HTTP Content-Type corresponding to the output format. May depend on the current format settings.

column.name: supports_random_access
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the format supports random access in the input.

column.name: has_schema_inference
column.Comment: The format can dynamically determine the schema from the data (either from embedded header/metadata or from the piece of data).

column.name: has_external_schema
column.Comment: The format either has a fixed schema or accepts a predefined schema in its own format.

column.name: prefers_large_blocks
column.Comment: The format will write larger blocks into output and generate larger blocks on input.

column.name: supports_append
column.Comment: It's possible to append into a single file with this format.

column.name: supports_subsets_of_columns
column.Comment: The input format can recognize when certain columns are omitted.

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of the function.

column.name: is_aggregate
column.Comment: Whether the function is an aggregate function.

column.name: case_insensitive
column.Comment: Whether the function name can be used case-insensitively.

column.name: alias_to
column.Comment: The original function name, if the function name is an alias.

column.name: create_query
column.Comment: Obsolete.

column.name: origin
column.Comment: Obsolete.

column.name: description
column.Comment: A high-level description what the function does.

column.name: syntax
column.Comment: Signature of the function.

column.name: arguments
column.Comment: The function arguments.

column.name: parameters
column.Comment: The function parameters (only for aggregate function).

column.name: returned_value
column.Comment: What does the function return.

column.name: examples
column.Comment: Usage example.

column.name: introduced_in
column.Comment: ClickHouse version in which the function was first introduced.

column.name: categories
column.Comment: The category of the function.

column.name: user_name
column.Comment: User name.

column.name: role_name
column.Comment: Role assigned to user account.

column.name: access_type
column.Comment: Access parameters for ClickHouse user account.

column.name: access_object
column.Comment: Parameter for access_type. Contains: 1 - Name of the source type for READ/WRITE grants.2 - Name of the table engine for TABLE ENGINE grants.3 - Name of the user for grants like `SET DEFINER`.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of a database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of a table.

column.name: column
column.Comment: Name of a column to which access is granted.

column.name: is_partial_revoke
column.Comment: Logical value. It shows whether some privileges have been revoked. Possible values: 0 — The row describes a grant, 1 — The row describes a partial revoke.

column.name: grant_option
column.Comment: Permission is granted WITH GRANT OPTION.

column.name: config_name
column.Comment: graphite_rollup parameter name.

column.name: rule_type
column.Comment: The rule type. Possible values: RuleTypeAll = 0 - default, with regex, compatible with old scheme; RuleTypePlain = 1 - plain metrics, with regex, compatible with old scheme; RuleTypeTagged = 2 - tagged metrics, with regex, compatible with old scheme; RuleTypeTagList = 3 - tagged metrics, with regex (converted to RuleTypeTagged from string like 'retention=10min ; env=(staging|prod)')

column.name: regexp
column.Comment: A pattern for the metric name.

column.name: function
column.Comment: The name of the aggregating function.

column.name: age
column.Comment: The minimum age of the data in seconds.

column.name: precision
column.Comment: How precisely to define the age of the data in seconds.

column.name: priority
column.Comment: Pattern priority.

column.name: is_default
column.Comment: Whether the pattern is the default.

column.name: Tables.database
column.Comment: Array of names of database tables that use the `config_name` parameter.

column.name: Tables.table
column.Comment: Array of table names that use the `config_name` parameter.

column.name: metric
column.Comment: Metric name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Metric value.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Metric description.

column.name: labels
column.Comment: Metric labels.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: made_current_at
column.Comment: Date & time when this snapshot was made current snapshot

column.name: snapshot_id
column.Comment: Snapshot id which is used to identify a snapshot.

column.name: parent_id
column.Comment: Parent id of this snapshot.

column.name: is_current_ancestor
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if this snapshot is an ancestor of the current snapshot.

column.name: id
column.Comment: ID of the instrumentation point

column.name: function_id
column.Comment: ID assigned to the function in xray_instr_map section of elf-binary.

column.name: function_name
column.Comment: Name used to instrument the function.

column.name: handler
column.Comment: Handler that was patched into instrumentation points of the function.

column.name: entry_type
column.Comment: Entry type for the patch.

column.name: symbol
column.Comment: Complete and demangled symbol name.

column.name: parameters
column.Comment: Parameters for the handler call.

column.name: index
column.Comment: Index of the bin ordered by size.

column.name: large
column.Comment: True for large allocations and False for small.

column.name: size
column.Comment: Size of allocations in this bin.

column.name: allocations
column.Comment: Number of allocations.

column.name: deallocations
column.Comment: Number of deallocations.

column.name: nregs
column.Comment: Number of regions per slab.

column.name: curslabs
column.Comment: Current number of slabs.

column.name: curregs
column.Comment: Current number of regions for this size class.

column.name: line
column.Comment: Line from the symbolized jemalloc heap profile.

column.name: stats
column.Comment: JEMalloc statistics output from malloc_stats_print.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database of the table with Kafka Engine.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table with Kafka Engine.

column.name: consumer_id
column.Comment: Kafka consumer identifier. Note, that a table can have many consumers. Specified by `kafka_num_consumers` parameter.

column.name: assignments.topic
column.Comment: Kafka topic.

column.name: assignments.partition_id
column.Comment: Kafka partition id. Note, that only one consumer can be assigned to a partition.

column.name: assignments.current_offset
column.Comment: Current offset.

column.name: assignments.intent_size
column.Comment: The number of pushed, but not yet committed messages in new StorageKafka.

column.name: exceptions.time
column.Comment: Timestamp when the 10 most recent exceptions were generated.

column.name: exceptions.text
column.Comment: Text of 10 most recent exceptions.

column.name: last_poll_time
column.Comment: Timestamp of the most recent poll.

column.name: num_messages_read
column.Comment: Number of messages read by the consumer.

column.name: last_commit_time
column.Comment: Timestamp of the most recent poll.

column.name: num_commits
column.Comment: Total number of commits for the consumer.

column.name: last_rebalance_time
column.Comment: Timestamp of the most recent Kafka rebalance.

column.name: num_rebalance_revocations
column.Comment: Number of times the consumer was revoked its partitions.

column.name: num_rebalance_assignments
column.Comment: Number of times the consumer was assigned to Kafka cluster.

column.name: is_currently_used
column.Comment: The flag which shows whether the consumer is in use.

column.name: last_used
column.Comment: The last time this consumer was in use.

column.name: rdkafka_stat
column.Comment: Library internal statistic. Set statistics_interval_ms to 0 disable, default is 3000 (once in three seconds).

column.name: dependencies
