column.name: CurrentMetric_DistributedBytesToInsert
column.Comment: Number of pending bytes to process for asynchronous insertion into Distributed tables. Number of bytes for every shard is summed.

column.name: CurrentMetric_BrokenDistributedBytesToInsert
column.Comment: Number of bytes for asynchronous insertion into Distributed tables that has been marked as broken. Number of bytes for every shard is summed.

column.Comment: Number of pending files to process for asynchronous insertion into Distributed tables. Number of files for every shard is summed.

column.name: CurrentMetric_BrokenDistributedFilesToInsert
column.Comment: Number of files for asynchronous insertion into Distributed tables that has been marked as broken. Number of files for every shard is summed.

column.name: CurrentMetric_TablesToDropQueueSize
column.Comment: Number of dropped tables, that are waiting for background data removal.

column.name: CurrentMetric_MaxDDLEntryID
column.Comment: Max processed DDL entry of DDLWorker.

column.name: CurrentMetric_MaxPushedDDLEntryID
column.Comment: Max DDL entry of DDLWorker that pushed to zookeeper.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsTemporary
column.Comment: The part is generating now, it is not in data_parts list.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsPreCommitted
column.Comment: Deprecated. See PartsPreActive.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsCommitted
column.Comment: Deprecated. See PartsActive.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsPreActive
column.Comment: The part is in data_parts, but not used for SELECTs.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsActive
column.Comment: Active data part, used by current and upcoming SELECTs.

column.name: CurrentMetric_AttachedDatabase
column.Comment: Active databases.

column.name: CurrentMetric_AttachedTable
column.Comment: Active tables.

column.name: CurrentMetric_AttachedReplicatedTable
column.Comment: Active replicated tables.

column.name: CurrentMetric_AttachedView
column.Comment: Active views.

column.name: CurrentMetric_AttachedDictionary
column.Comment: Active dictionaries.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsOutdated
column.Comment: Not active data part, but could be used by only current SELECTs, could be deleted after SELECTs finishes.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsDeleting
column.Comment: Not active data part with identity refcounter, it is deleting right now by a cleaner.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsDeleteOnDestroy
column.Comment: Part was moved to another disk and should be deleted in own destructor.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsWide
column.Comment: Wide parts.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PartsCompact
column.Comment: Compact parts.

column.name: CurrentMetric_MMappedFiles
column.Comment: Total number of mmapped files.

column.name: CurrentMetric_MMappedFileBytes
column.Comment: Sum size of mmapped file regions.

column.name: CurrentMetric_AsynchronousReadWait
column.Comment: Number of threads waiting for asynchronous read.

column.name: CurrentMetric_PendingAsyncInsert
column.Comment: Number of asynchronous inserts that are waiting for flush.

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaConsumers
column.Comment: Number of active Kafka consumers

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaConsumersWithAssignment
column.Comment: Number of active Kafka consumers which have some partitions assigned.

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaProducers
column.Comment: Number of active Kafka producer created

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaLibrdkafkaThreads
column.Comment: Number of active librdkafka threads

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaBackgroundReads
column.Comment: Number of background reads currently working (populating materialized views from Kafka)

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaConsumersInUse
column.Comment: Number of consumers which are currently used by direct or background reads

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaWrites
column.Comment: Number of currently running inserts to Kafka

column.name: CurrentMetric_KafkaAssignedPartitions
column.Comment: Number of partitions Kafka tables currently assigned to

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheReadBuffers
column.Comment: Number of active cache buffers

column.name: CurrentMetric_CacheFileSegments
column.Comment: Number of existing cache file segments

column.name: CurrentMetric_CacheDetachedFileSegments
column.Comment: Number of existing detached cache file segments

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheSize
column.Comment: Filesystem cache size in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheSizeLimit
column.Comment: Filesystem cache size limit in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheElements
column.Comment: Filesystem cache elements (file segments)

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheDownloadQueueElements
column.Comment: Filesystem cache elements in download queue

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheDelayedCleanupElements
column.Comment: Filesystem cache elements in background cleanup queue

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheHoldFileSegments
column.Comment: Filesystem cache file segment which are currently hold as unreleasable

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheKeys
column.Comment: Number of keys in filesystem cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilesystemCacheReserveThreads
column.Comment: Threads number trying to reserve space in cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_AsyncInsertCacheSize
column.Comment: Number of async insert hash id in cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_IcebergMetadataFilesCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the Iceberg metadata cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_IcebergMetadataFilesCacheFiles
column.Comment: Number of cached files in the Iceberg metadata cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_AvroSchemaCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the Avro schema cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_AvroSchemaCacheCells
column.Comment: Number of cached Avro schemas

column.name: CurrentMetric_AvroSchemaRegistryCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the Avro schema registry cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_AvroSchemaRegistryCacheCells
column.Comment: Number of entries in Avro schema registry cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_HiveFilesCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the hive cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_HiveFilesCacheFiles
column.Comment: Number of cached files in the hive cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_HiveMetadataFilesCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the hive metadata cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_HiveMetadataFilesCacheFiles
column.Comment: Number of cached files in the hive metadata cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_VectorSimilarityIndexCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the vector similarity index cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_VectorSimilarityIndexCacheCells
column.Comment: Number of entries in the vector similarity index cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_TextIndexDictionaryBlockCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the text index dictionary block cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_TextIndexDictionaryBlockCacheCells
column.Comment: Number of entries in the text index dictionary block cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_TextIndexHeaderCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the text index header cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_TextIndexHeaderCacheCells
column.Comment: Number of entries in text index header cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_TextIndexPostingsCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the text index posting lists cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_TextIndexPostingsCacheCells
column.Comment: Number of entries in the text index posting lists cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_DNSHostsCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the DNS hosts cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_DNSHostsCacheSize
column.Comment: Number of cached DNS hosts

column.name: CurrentMetric_DNSAddressesCacheBytes
column.Comment: Size of the DNS addresses cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_DNSAddressesCacheSize
column.Comment: Number of cached DNS addresses

column.name: CurrentMetric_MarkCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of mark cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_MarkCacheFiles
column.Comment: Total number of mark files cached in the mark cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_NamedCollection
column.Comment: Number of named collections

column.name: CurrentMetric_PrimaryIndexCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of primary index cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_PrimaryIndexCacheFiles
column.Comment: Total number of index files cached in the primary index cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_PageCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of userspace page cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_PageCacheCells
column.Comment: Total number of entries in the userspace page cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_UncompressedCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed cache in bytes. Uncompressed cache does not usually improve the performance and should be mostly avoided

column.name: CurrentMetric_UncompressedCacheCells
column.Comment: Total number of entries in the uncompressed cache. Each entry represents a decompressed block of data. Uncompressed cache does not usually improve performance and should be mostly avoided

column.name: CurrentMetric_IndexMarkCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of mark cache for secondary indices in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_IndexMarkCacheFiles
column.Comment: Total number of mark files cached in the mark cache for secondary indices

column.name: CurrentMetric_IndexUncompressedCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed cache in bytes for secondary indices. Uncompressed cache does not usually improve the performance and should be mostly avoided

column.name: CurrentMetric_IndexUncompressedCacheCells
column.Comment: Total number of entries in the uncompressed cache for secondary indices. Each entry represents a decompressed block of data. Uncompressed cache does not usually improve performance and should be mostly avoided

column.name: CurrentMetric_MMapCacheCells
column.Comment: The number of files opened with `mmap` (mapped in memory). This is used for queries with the setting `local_filesystem_read_method` set to `mmap`. The files opened with `mmap` are kept in the cache to avoid costly TLB flushes.

column.name: CurrentMetric_QueryCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of the query cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_QueryCacheEntries
column.Comment: Total number of entries in the query cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_QueryConditionCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of the query condition cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_QueryConditionCacheEntries
column.Comment: Total number of entries in the query condition cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_CompiledExpressionCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total bytes used for the cache of JIT-compiled code

column.name: CurrentMetric_CompiledExpressionCacheCount
column.Comment: Total entries in the cache of JIT-compiled code

column.name: CurrentMetric_MergeJoinBlocksCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total bytes used for cached blocks in MergeJoin

column.name: CurrentMetric_MergeJoinBlocksCacheCount
column.Comment: Total cached blocks in MergeJoin

column.name: CurrentMetric_BcryptCacheBytes
column.Comment: Total size of the bcrypt authentication cache in bytes

column.name: CurrentMetric_BcryptCacheSize
column.Comment: Total number of entries in the bcrypt authentication cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_ColumnsDescriptionsCacheSize
column.Comment: Size of ColumnsDescriptions cache (per-table cache)

column.name: CurrentMetric_S3Requests
column.Comment: S3 requests count

column.name: CurrentMetric_KeeperAliveConnections
column.Comment: Number of alive connections

column.name: CurrentMetric_KeeperOutstandingRequests
column.Comment: Number of outstanding requests

column.name: CurrentMetric_ThreadsInOvercommitTracker
column.Comment: Number of waiting threads inside of OvercommitTracker

column.name: CurrentMetric_IOUringPendingEvents
column.Comment: Number of io_uring SQEs waiting to be submitted

column.name: CurrentMetric_IOUringInFlightEvents
column.Comment: Number of io_uring SQEs in flight

column.name: CurrentMetric_ReadTaskRequestsSent
column.Comment: The current number of callback requests in flight from the remote server back to the initiator server to choose the read task (for s3Cluster table function and similar). Measured on the remote server side.

column.name: CurrentMetric_MergeTreeReadTaskRequestsSent
column.Comment: The current number of callback requests in flight from the remote server back to the initiator server to choose the read task (for MergeTree tables). Measured on the remote server side.

column.name: CurrentMetric_MergeTreeAllRangesAnnouncementsSent
column.Comment: The current number of announcement being sent in flight from the remote server to the initiator server about the set of data parts (for MergeTree tables). Measured on the remote server side.

column.name: CurrentMetric_CreatedTimersInQueryProfiler
column.Comment: Number of Created thread local timers in QueryProfiler

column.name: CurrentMetric_ActiveTimersInQueryProfiler
column.Comment: Number of Active thread local timers in QueryProfiler

column.name: CurrentMetric_RefreshableViews
column.Comment: Number materialized views with periodic refreshing (REFRESH)

column.name: CurrentMetric_RefreshingViews
column.Comment: Number of materialized views currently executing a refresh

column.name: CurrentMetric_StorageBufferFlushThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads for background flushes in StorageBuffer

column.name: CurrentMetric_StorageBufferFlushThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of threads for background flushes in StorageBuffer running a task

column.name: CurrentMetric_StorageBufferFlushThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active threads for background flushes in StorageBuffer

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the thread pools in internals of SharedMergeTree

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of threads in the thread pools in internals of SharedMergeTree running a task

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active threads in the thread pools in internals of SharedMergeTree

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeFetch
column.Comment: Number of fetches in progress

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeAssignedCurrentParts
column.Comment: Number of parts locked by merge or mutation

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeOutdatedPartsInKeeper
column.Comment: How many outdated part records stored in keeper

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeCondemnedPartsInKeeper
column.Comment: How many condemned part records stored in keeper

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeBrokenCondemnedPartsInKeeper
column.Comment: How many broken condemned part records stored in keeper

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMaxActiveReplicas
column.Comment: The maximum number of active replicas registered in Keeper

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMaxInactiveReplicas
column.Comment: The maximum number of inactive replicas registered in Keeper

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMaxReplicas
column.Comment: The maximum number of replicas registered in Keeper across all tables. Note it might not be a sum of SharedMergeTreeMaxActiveReplicas and SharedMergeTreeMaxInactiveReplicas

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMinActiveReplicas
column.Comment: The minimum number of active replicas registered in Keeper

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMinInactiveReplicas
column.Comment: The minimum number of inactive replicas registered in Keeper

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMinReplicas
column.Comment: The minimum number of replicas registered in Keeper across all tables. Note it might not be a sum of SharedMergeTreeMinActiveReplicas and SharedMergeTreeMinInactiveReplicas

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMinPartitions
column.Comment: The minimum number of partitions registered in Keeper across all SharedMergeTree tables

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedMergeTreeMaxPartitions
column.Comment: The maximum number of partitions registered in Keeper across all SharedMergeTree tables

column.name: CurrentMetric_CacheWarmerBytesInProgress
column.Comment: Total size of remote file segments waiting to be asynchronously loaded into filesystem cache.

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheOpenedConnections
column.Comment: Number of open connections to Distributed Cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheSharedLimitCount
column.Comment: Number of opened connections according to DistributedCache::ConnectionPool::SharedLimit

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheUsedConnections
column.Comment: Number of currently used connections to Distributed Cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheAllocatedConnections
column.Comment: Number of currently allocated connections to Distributed Cache connection pool

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheBorrowedConnections
column.Comment: Number of currently borrowed connections to Distributed Cache connection pool

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheReadRequests
column.Comment: Number of executed Read requests to Distributed Cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheWriteRequests
column.Comment: Number of executed Write requests to Distributed Cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheWriteBuffers
column.Comment: Number of distributed cache write buffers

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheReadBuffers
column.Comment: Number of distributed cache read buffers

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheServerConnections
column.Comment: Number of open connections to ClickHouse server from Distributed Cache

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheRegisteredServers
column.Comment: Number of distributed cache registered servers

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheRegisteredServersCurrentAZ
column.Comment: Number of distributed cache registered servers in current az

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheServerS3CachedClients
column.Comment: Number of distributed cache S3 cached clients

column.name: CurrentMetric_DistrCacheServerRegistryConnections
column.Comment: Number of active connections to ClickHouse server from Distributed Cache (not marked as cancelled because of limit)

column.name: CurrentMetric_SchedulerIOReadScheduled
column.Comment: Number of IO reads are being scheduled currently

column.name: CurrentMetric_SchedulerIOWriteScheduled
column.Comment: Number of IO writes are being scheduled currently

column.name: CurrentMetric_StorageConnectionsStored
column.Comment: Total count of sessions stored in the session pool for storages

column.name: CurrentMetric_StorageConnectionsTotal
column.Comment: Total count of all sessions: stored in the pool and actively used right now for storages

column.name: CurrentMetric_DiskConnectionsStored
column.Comment: Total count of sessions stored in the session pool for disks

column.name: CurrentMetric_DiskConnectionsTotal
column.Comment: Total count of all sessions: stored in the pool and actively used right now for disks

column.name: CurrentMetric_BlobKillerThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the thread pool of the object storage disk background removal process

column.name: CurrentMetric_BlobKillerThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of threads in the thread pool of the object storage disk background removal process running a task

column.name: CurrentMetric_BlobKillerThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active tasks in the thread pool of the object storage disk background removal process

column.name: CurrentMetric_BlobCopierThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the thread pool of the object storage disk background replication process

column.name: CurrentMetric_BlobCopierThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of threads in the thread pool of the object storage disk background replication process running a task

column.name: CurrentMetric_BlobCopierThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active tasks in the thread pool of the object storage disk background replication process

column.name: CurrentMetric_HTTPConnectionsStored
column.Comment: Total count of sessions stored in the session pool for http hosts

column.name: CurrentMetric_HTTPConnectionsTotal
column.Comment: Total count of all sessions: stored in the pool and actively used right now for http hosts

column.name: CurrentMetric_AddressesActive
column.Comment: Total count of addresses which are used for creation connections with connection pools

column.name: CurrentMetric_AddressesBanned
column.Comment: Total count of addresses which are banned as faulty for creation connections with connection pools

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilteringMarksWithPrimaryKey
column.Comment: Number of threads currently doing filtering of mark ranges by the primary key

column.name: CurrentMetric_FilteringMarksWithSecondaryKeys
column.Comment: Number of threads currently doing filtering of mark ranges by secondary keys

column.name: CurrentMetric_ConcurrencyControlScheduled
column.Comment: Total number of CPU slot requests are being scheduled currently

column.name: CurrentMetric_ConcurrencyControlAcquired
column.Comment: Total number of acquired CPU slots

column.name: CurrentMetric_ConcurrencyControlAcquiredNonCompeting
column.Comment: Total number of acquired CPU slots that are not considered competing (the first thread if fair_round_robin scheduler is in use)

column.name: CurrentMetric_ConcurrencyControlSoftLimit
column.Comment: Value of soft limit on number of CPU slots

column.name: CurrentMetric_ConcurrencyControlPreempted
column.Comment: Total number of preempted threads waiting for CPU slot

column.name: CurrentMetric_ConcurrentQueryScheduled
column.Comment: Total number of query slot requests are being scheduled currently

column.name: CurrentMetric_ConcurrentQueryAcquired
column.Comment: Total number of acquired query slots

column.name: CurrentMetric_DiskS3NoSuchKeyErrors
column.Comment: The number of `NoSuchKey` errors that occur when reading data from S3 cloud storage through ClickHouse disks.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogStateApplicationThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the threadpool for state application in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogStateApplicationThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of active threads in the threadpool for state application in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogStateApplicationThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active jobs in the threadpool for state application in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogDropLocalThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the threadpool for drop of local tables in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogDropLocalThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of active threads in the threadpool for drop of local tables in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogDropLocalThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active jobs in the threadpool for drop of local tables in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogDropZooKeeperThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the threadpool for drop of object in ZooKeeper in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogDropZooKeeperThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of active threads in the threadpool for drop of object in ZooKeeper in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogDropZooKeeperThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active jobs in the threadpool for drop of object in ZooKeeper in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_CoordinatedMergesCoordinatorRunningMerges
column.Comment: Number of running merges visible for merge coordinator.

column.name: CurrentMetric_CoordinatedMergesCoordinatorAssignedMerges
column.Comment: Number of assigned merges in merge coordinator state.

column.name: CurrentMetric_CoordinatedMergesWorkerAssignedMerges
column.Comment: Number of assigned merges in merge worker state.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedDatabaseCatalogTablesInLocalDropDetachQueue
column.Comment: Number of tables in the queue for local drop or detach in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogDropDetachLocalTablesErrors
column.Comment: Number of errors that occurred when attempting to drop or detach local tables in Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_SharedCatalogNumberOfObjectsInState
column.Comment: Number of objects in the current state of Shared Catalog.

column.name: CurrentMetric_MetadataFromKeeperCacheObjects
column.Comment: Number of objects in disk metadata cache.

column.name: CurrentMetric_LicenseRemainingSeconds
column.Comment: Remaining seconds of the license validity period.

column.name: CurrentMetric_StartupScriptsExecutionState
column.Comment: State of startup scripts execution: 0 = not finished, 1 = success, 2 = failure.

column.name: CurrentMetric_IsServerShuttingDown
column.Comment: Indicates if the server is shutting down: 0 = no, 1 = yes

column.name: CurrentMetric_StatelessWorkerThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the stateless worker thread pool.

column.name: CurrentMetric_StatelessWorkerThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of threads in the stateless worker thread pool running a task.

column.name: CurrentMetric_StatelessWorkerThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active jobs in the stateless worker thread pool.

column.name: CurrentMetric_ReadonlyDisks
column.Comment: Number of disks that were marked as readonly during disk check.

column.name: CurrentMetric_BrokenDisks
column.Comment: Number of disks disks that were marked as broken during disk check.

column.name: CurrentMetric_TaskTrackerThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads used by the distributed query remote task tracker.

column.name: CurrentMetric_TaskTrackerThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of threads in the distributed query remote task tracker thread pool running a task.

column.name: CurrentMetric_TaskTrackerThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active jobs in the distributed query remote task tracker thread pool.

column.name: CurrentMetric_DropDistributedCacheThreads
column.Comment: Number of threads in the threadpool for drop distributed cache query.

column.name: CurrentMetric_DropDistributedCacheThreadsActive
column.Comment: Number of active threads in the threadpool for drop distributed cache query.

column.name: CurrentMetric_DropDistributedCacheThreadsScheduled
column.Comment: Number of queued or active jobs in the threadpool for drop distributed cache.

column.name: CurrentMetric_S3CachedCredentialsProviders
column.Comment: Total number of cached credentials providers

column.name: metric
column.Comment: Metric name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Metric value.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Metric description.

column.name: model_path
column.Comment: Path to trained model.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Model type. Now catboost only.

column.name: loading_start_time
column.Comment: The time when the loading of the model started.

column.name: loading_duration
column.Comment: How much time did it take to load the model.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table containing moving data part.

column.name: elapsed
column.Comment: Time elapsed (in seconds) since data part movement started.

column.name: target_disk_name
column.Comment: Name of disk to which the data part is moving.

column.name: target_disk_path
column.Comment: Path to the mount point of the disk in the file system.

column.name: part_name
column.Comment: Name of the data part being moved.

column.name: part_size
column.Comment: Data part size.

column.name: thread_id
column.Comment: Identifier of a thread performing the movement.

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the database to which the mutation was applied.

column.name: table
column.Comment: The name of the table to which the mutation was applied.

column.name: mutation_id
column.Comment: The ID of the mutation. For replicated tables these IDs correspond to znode names in the <table_path_in_clickhouse_keeper>/mutations/ directory in ClickHouse Keeper. For non-replicated tables the IDs correspond to file names in the data directory of the table.

column.name: command
column.Comment: The mutation command string (the part of the query after ALTER TABLE [db.]table).

column.name: create_time
column.Comment: Date and time when the mutation command was submitted for execution.

column.name: block_numbers.partition_id
column.Comment: For mutations of replicated tables, the array contains the partitions' IDs (one record for each partition). For mutations of non-replicated tables the array is empty.

column.name: block_numbers.number
column.Comment: For mutations of replicated tables, the array contains one record for each partition, with the block number that was acquired by the mutation. Only parts that contain blocks with numbers less than this number will be mutated in the partition.In non-replicated tables, block numbers in all partitions form a single sequence. This means that for mutations of non-replicated tables, the column will contain one record with a single block number acquired by the mutation.

column.name: parts_in_progress_names
column.Comment: An array of names of data parts that are currently being mutated.

column.name: parts_to_do_names
column.Comment: An array of names of data parts that need to be mutated for the mutation to complete.

column.name: parts_to_do
column.Comment: The number of data parts that need to be mutated for the mutation to complete.

column.name: parts_postpone_reasons
column.Comment: A map of part names to reasons why they are postponed.

column.name: is_done
column.Comment: The flag whether the mutation is done or not. Possible values: 1 if the mutation is completed, 0 if the mutation is still in process.

column.name: is_killed
column.Comment: Indicates whether a mutation has been killed. Only available in ClickHouse Cloud.Note: is_killed=1 does not necessarily mean the mutation is completely finalized.It is possible for a mutation to remain in a state where is_killed=1 and is_done=0 for an extended period.This can occur if another long-running mutation is blocking the killed mutation. This is a normal situation.

column.name: latest_failed_part
column.Comment: The name of the most recent part that could not be mutated.

column.name: latest_fail_time
column.Comment: The date and time of the most recent part mutation failure.

column.name: latest_fail_reason
column.Comment: The exception message that caused the most recent part mutation failure.

column.name: latest_fail_error_code_name
column.Comment: The error code of the exception that caused the most recent part mutation failure.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the collection.

column.name: collection
column.Comment: Collection internals.

column.name: source
column.Comment: Named collection source.

column.name: create_query
column.Comment: Named collection create query.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: Identifier of the INSERT query that created this data part.

column.name: event_type
column.Comment: Type of the event that occurred with the data part. Can have one of the following values: NewPart — Inserting of a new data part, MergePartsStart — Merging of data parts has started, MergeParts — Merging of data parts has finished, DownloadPart — Downloading a data part, RemovePart — Removing or detaching a data part using [DETACH PARTITION](/sql-reference/statements/alter/partition#detach-partitionpart).MutatePartStart — Mutating of a data part has started, MutatePart — Mutating of a data part has finished, MovePart — Moving the data part from the one disk to another one.

column.name: merge_reason
column.Comment: The reason for the event with type MERGE_PARTS. Can have one of the following values: NotAMerge — The current event has the type other than MERGE_PARTS, RegularMerge — Some regular merge, TTLDeleteMerge, TTLDropMerge — Cleaning up expired data. TTLRecompressMerge — Recompressing data part with the.

column.name: merge_algorithm
column.Comment: Merge algorithm for the event with type MERGE_PARTS. Can have one of the following values: Undecided, Horizontal, Vertical

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: Event date.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: Event time.

column.name: event_time_microseconds
column.Comment: Event time with microseconds precision.

column.name: duration_ms
column.Comment: Duration of this operation.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database the data part is in.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table the data part is in.

column.name: table_uuid
column.Comment: UUID of the table the data part belongs to.

column.name: part_name
column.Comment: Name of the data part.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: ID of the partition that the data part was inserted to. The column takes the `all` value if the partitioning is by `tuple()`.

column.name: partition
column.Comment: The partition name.

column.name: part_type
column.Comment: The type of the part. Possible values: Wide and Compact.

column.name: part_storage_type
column.Comment: The type of DataPartStorage. Possible values: Packed - all files are stored in a single blob, Full - a blob per file.

column.name: disk_name
column.Comment: The disk name data part lies on.

column.name: path_on_disk
column.Comment: Absolute path to the folder with data part files.

column.name: rows
column.Comment: The number of rows in the data part.

column.name: size_in_bytes
column.Comment: Size of the data part on disk in bytes.

column.name: merged_from
column.Comment: An array of the source parts names which the current part was made up from.

column.name: bytes_uncompressed
column.Comment: Uncompressed size of the resulting part in bytes.

column.name: read_rows
column.Comment: The number of rows was read during the merge.

column.name: read_bytes
column.Comment: The number of bytes was read during the merge.

column.name: peak_memory_usage
column.Comment: The maximum amount of used during merge RAM

column.name: deduplication_block_ids
column.Comment: An array of block IDs used for deduplication when inserting this part.

column.name: error
column.Comment: The error code of the occurred exception.

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Text message of the occurred error.

column.name: mutation_ids
column.Comment: An array of mutation IDs applied to the source part (merged_from) for the event with type MUTATE_PART_START and MUTATE_PART.

column.name: ProfileEvents
column.Comment: All the profile events captured during this operation.

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the database where move is performed.

column.name: table
column.Comment: The name of the table where move is performed.

column.name: task_name
column.Comment: The name of the moving task.

column.name: task_uuid
column.Comment: The identifier of the moving task.

column.name: create_time
column.Comment: The time when the task was created.

column.name: part_name
column.Comment: The name of the part which is in a process of moving.

column.name: part_uuid
column.Comment: The UUID of the part which is in a process of moving.

column.name: to_shard
column.Comment: The name of the destination shard.

column.name: dst_part_name
column.Comment: The result part name.

column.name: update_time
column.Comment: The last time update was performed.

column.name: state
column.Comment: The current state of the move.

column.name: rollback
column.Comment: The flag which indicated whether the operation was rolled back.

column.name: num_tries
column.Comment: The number of tries to complete the operation.

column.name: last_exception
column.Comment: The last exception name if any.

column.name: partition
column.Comment: The partition name.

column.name: name
column.Comment:
Name of the data part. The part naming structure can be used to determine many aspects of the data, ingest, and merge patterns. The part naming format is the following:
``` <partition_id>_<minimum_block_number>_<maximum_block_number>_<level>_<data_version> ```
* Definitions: - `partition_id` - identifies the partition key - `minimum_block_number` - identifies the minimum block number in the part. ClickHouse always merges continuous blocks - `maximum_block_number` - identifies the maximum block number in the part - `level` - incremented by one with each additional merge on the part. A level of 0 indicates this is a new part that has not been merged. It is important to remember that all parts in ClickHouse are always immutable - `data_version` - optional value, incremented when a part is mutated (again, mutated data is always only written to a new part, since parts are immutable)

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: The UUID of data part.

column.name: part_type
column.Comment: The data part storing format. Possible Values: Wide (a file per column) and Compact (a single file for all columns).

column.name: active
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the data part is active. If a data part is active, it's used in a table. Otherwise, it's about to be deleted. Inactive data parts appear after merging and mutating operations.

column.name: marks
column.Comment: The number of marks. To get the approximate number of rows in a data part, multiply marks by the index granularity (usually 8192) (this hint does not work for adaptive granularity).

column.name: rows
column.Comment: The number of rows.

column.name: files
column.Comment: The number of files in the data part.

column.name: bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the data part files in bytes.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: primary_key_size
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by primary key values in the primary.idx/cidx file on disk.

column.name: marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks.

column.name: secondary_indices_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data for secondary indices in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: secondary_indices_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data for secondary indices in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: secondary_indices_marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks for secondary indices.

column.name: modification_time
column.Comment: The time the directory with the data part was modified. This usually corresponds to the time of data part creation.

column.name: remove_time
column.Comment: The time when the data part became inactive.

column.name: refcount
column.Comment: The number of places where the data part is used. A value greater than 2 indicates that the data part is used in queries or merges.

column.name: min_date
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date key in the data part.

column.name: max_date
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date key in the data part.

column.name: min_time
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date and time key in the data part.

column.name: max_time
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date and time key in the data part.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: ID of the partition.

column.name: min_block_number
column.Comment: The minimum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: max_block_number
column.Comment: The maximum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Depth of the merge tree. Zero means that the current part was created by insert rather than by merging other parts.

column.name: data_version
column.Comment: Number that is used to determine which mutations should be applied to the data part (mutations with a version higher than data_version).

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by primary key values.

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory_allocated
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) reserved for primary key values.

column.name: index_granularity_bytes_in_memory
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by index granularity values (will be 0 in case of primary_key_lazy_load=1 and use_primary_key_cache=1).

column.name: index_granularity_bytes_in_memory_allocated
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) reserved for index granularity values (will be 0 in case of primary_key_lazy_load=1 and use_primary_key_cache=1).

column.name: is_frozen
column.Comment: Flag that shows that a partition data backup exists. 1, the backup exists. 0, the backup does not exist.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Name of the table engine without parameters.

column.name: disk_name
column.Comment: Name of a disk that stores the data part.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Absolute path to the folder with data part files.

column.name: hash_of_all_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of compressed files.

column.name: hash_of_uncompressed_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of uncompressed files (files with marks, index file etc.).

column.name: uncompressed_hash_of_compressed_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of data in the compressed files as if they were uncompressed.

column.name: delete_ttl_info_min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date and time key for TTL DELETE rule.

column.name: delete_ttl_info_max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date and time key for TTL DELETE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: Array of expressions. Each expression defines a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: Array of date and time values. Each element describes the minimum key value for a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: Array of date and time values. Each element describes the maximum key value for a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: default_compression_codec
column.Comment: The name of the codec used to compress this data part (in case when there is no explicit codec for columns).

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: projections
column.Comment: The list of projection names calculated for this part.

column.name: visible
column.Comment: Flag which indicated whether this part is visible for SELECT queries.

column.name: creation_tid
column.Comment: ID of transaction that has created/is trying to create this object.

column.name: removal_tid_lock
column.Comment: Hash of removal_tid, used to lock an object for removal.

column.name: removal_tid
column.Comment: ID of transaction that has removed/is trying to remove this object

column.name: creation_csn
column.Comment: CSN of transaction that has created this object

column.name: removal_csn
column.Comment: CSN of transaction that has removed this object

column.name: has_lightweight_delete
column.Comment: The flag which indicated whether the part has lightweight delete mask.

column.name: last_removal_attempt_time
column.Comment: The last time the server tried to delete this part.

column.name: removal_state
column.Comment: The current state of part removal process.

column.name: partition
column.Comment: The partition name.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the data part.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: The parts UUID.

column.name: part_type
column.Comment: The data part storing format. Possible values: Wide — Each column is stored in a separate file in a filesystem, Compact — All columns are stored in one file in a filesystem.

column.name: active
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the data part is active. If a data part is active, it's used in a table. Otherwise, it's deleted. Inactive data parts remain after merging.

column.name: marks
column.Comment: The number of marks. To get the approximate number of rows in a data part, multiply marks by the index granularity (usually 8192) (this hint does not work for adaptive granularity).

column.name: rows
column.Comment: The number of rows.

column.name: bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the data part files in bytes.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks.

column.name: modification_time
column.Comment: The time the directory with the data part was modified. This usually corresponds to the time of data part creation.

column.name: remove_time
column.Comment: The time when the data part became inactive.

column.name: refcount
column.Comment: The number of places where the data part is used. A value greater than 2 indicates that the data part is used in queries or merges.

column.name: min_date
column.Comment: The minimum value for the Date column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: max_date
column.Comment: The maximum value for the Date column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: min_time
column.Comment: The minimum value for the DateTime column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: max_time
column.Comment: The maximum value for the DateTime column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: ID of the partition.

column.name: min_block_number
column.Comment: The minimum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: max_block_number
column.Comment: The maximum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Depth of the merge tree. Zero means that the current part was created by insert rather than by merging other parts.

column.name: data_version
column.Comment: Number that is used to determine which mutations should be applied to the data part (mutations with a version higher than data_version).

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by primary key values.

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory_allocated
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) reserved for primary key values.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Name of the table engine without parameters.

column.name: disk_name
column.Comment: Name of a disk that stores the data part.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Absolute path to the folder with data part files.

column.name: column
column.Comment: Name of the column.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Column type.

column.name: column_position
column.Comment: Ordinal position of a column in a table starting with 1.

column.name: default_kind
column.Comment: Expression type (DEFAULT, MATERIALIZED, ALIAS) for the default value, or an empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: default_expression
column.Comment: Expression for the default value, or an empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: column_bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of the column in bytes.

column.name: column_data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of the compressed data in the column, in bytes.

column.name: column_data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of the decompressed data in the column, in bytes.

column.name: column_marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the marks for column, in bytes.

column.name: column_modification_time
column.Comment: The last time the column was modified.

column.name: column_ttl_min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression of the column.

column.name: column_ttl_max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression of the column.

column.name: statistics
column.Comment: The statistics of the column.

column.name: estimates.min
column.Comment: Estimated minimum value of the column.

column.name: estimates.max
column.Comment: Estimated maximum value of the column.

column.name: estimates.cardinality
column.Comment: Estimated cardinality of the column.

column.name: serialization_kind
column.Comment: Kind of serialization of a column

column.name: substreams
column.Comment: Names of substreams to which column is serialized

column.name: filenames
column.Comment: Names of files for each substream of a column respectively

column.name: subcolumns.names
column.Comment: Names of subcolumns of a column

column.name: subcolumns.types
column.Comment: Types of subcolumns of a column

column.name: subcolumns.serializations
column.Comment: Kinds of serialization of subcolumns of a column

column.name: subcolumns.bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Sizes in bytes for each subcolumn

column.name: subcolumns.data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Sizes of the compressed data for each subcolumn, in bytes

column.name: subcolumns.data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Sizes of the decompressed data for each subcolumn, in bytes

column.name: subcolumns.marks_bytes
column.Comment: Sizes of the marks for each subcolumn of a column, in bytes

column.name: privilege
column.Comment: Name of a privilege which can be used in the GRANT command.

column.name: aliases
column.Comment: List of aliases which can be used instead of the name of the privilege.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Level of the privilege. GLOBAL privileges can be granted only globally (ON *.*), DATABASE privileges can be granted on a specific database (ON <database>.*) or globally (ON *.*), TABLE privileges can be granted either on a specific table or on a specific database or globally, and COLUMN privileges can be granted like TABLE privileges but also allow to specify columns.

column.name: parent_group
column.Comment: Parent privilege - if the parent privilege is granted then all its children privileges are considered as granted too.

column.name: is_initial_query
column.Comment: Whether this query comes directly from user or was issues by ClickHouse server in a scope of distributed query execution.

column.name: user
column.Comment: The user who made the query. Keep in mind that for distributed processing, queries are sent to remote servers under the default user. The field contains the username for a specific query, not for a query that this query initiated.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: Query ID, if defined.

column.name: address
column.Comment: The IP address the query was made from. The same for distributed processing. To track where a distributed query was originally made from, look at system.processes on the query requestor server.

column.name: port
column.Comment: The client port the query was made from.

column.name: initial_user
column.Comment: Name of the user who ran the initial query (for distributed query execution).

column.name: initial_query_id
column.Comment: ID of the initial query (for distributed query execution).

column.name: initial_address
column.Comment: IP address that the parent query was launched from.

column.name: initial_port
column.Comment: The client port that was used to make the parent query.

column.name: interface
column.Comment: The interface which was used to send the query. TCP = 1, HTTP = 2, GRPC = 3, MYSQL = 4, POSTGRESQL = 5, LOCAL = 6, TCP_INTERSERVER = 7.

column.name: os_user
column.Comment: Operating system username who runs clickhouse-client.

column.name: client_hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the client machine where the clickhouse-client or another TCP client is run.

column.name: client_name
column.Comment: The clickhouse-client or another TCP client name.

column.name: client_revision
column.Comment: Revision of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client.

column.name: client_version_major
column.Comment: Major version of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client.

column.name: client_version_minor
column.Comment: Minor version of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client.

column.name: client_version_patch
column.Comment: Patch component of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client version.

column.name: http_method
column.Comment: HTTP method that initiated the query. Possible values: 0 — The query was launched from the TCP interface. 1 — GET method was used. 2 — POST method was used.

column.name: http_user_agent
column.Comment: HTTP header UserAgent passed in the HTTP query.

column.name: http_referer
column.Comment: HTTP header Referer passed in the HTTP query (contains an absolute or partial address of the page making the query).

column.name: forwarded_for
column.Comment: HTTP header X-Forwarded-For passed in the HTTP query.

column.name: quota_key
column.Comment: The quota key specified in the quotas setting (see keyed).

column.name: distributed_depth
column.Comment: The number of times query was retransmitted between server nodes internally.

column.name: elapsed
column.Comment: The time in seconds since request execution started.

column.name: is_cancelled
column.Comment: Was query cancelled.

column.name: is_all_data_sent
column.Comment: Was all data sent to the client (in other words query had been finished on the server).

column.name: read_rows
column.Comment: The number of rows read from the table. For distributed processing, on the requestor server, this is the total for all remote servers.

column.name: read_bytes
column.Comment: The number of uncompressed bytes read from the table. For distributed processing, on the requestor server, this is the total for all remote servers.

column.name: total_rows_approx
column.Comment: The approximation of the total number of rows that should be read. For distributed processing, on the requestor server, this is the total for all remote servers. It can be updated during request processing, when new sources to process become known.

column.name: written_rows
column.Comment: The amount of rows written to the storage.

column.name: written_bytes
column.Comment: The amount of bytes written to the storage.

column.name: memory_usage
column.Comment: Amount of RAM the query uses. It might not include some types of dedicated memory

column.name: peak_memory_usage
column.Comment: The current peak of memory usage.

column.name: query
column.Comment: The query text. For INSERT, it does not include the data to insert.

column.name: normalized_query_hash
column.Comment: A numeric hash value, such as it is identical for queries differ only by values of literals.

column.name: query_kind
column.Comment: The type of the query - SELECT, INSERT, etc.

column.name: thread_ids
column.Comment: The list of identifiers of all threads which participated in this query.

column.name: peak_threads_usage
column.Comment: Maximum count of simultaneous threads executing the query.

column.name: ProfileEvents
column.Comment: ProfileEvents calculated for this query.

column.name: Settings
column.Comment: The list of modified user-level settings.

column.name: current_database
column.Comment: The name of the current database.

column.name: is_internal
column.Comment: Indicates whether it is an auxiliary query executed internally.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: The date when the event happened.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: The date and time when the event happened.

column.name: event_time_microseconds
column.Comment: The date and time with microseconds precision when the event happened.

column.name: id
column.Comment: ID of processor.

column.name: parent_ids
column.Comment: Parent processors IDs.

column.name: plan_step
column.Comment: ID of the query plan step which created this processor. The value is zero if the processor was not added from any step.

column.name: plan_step_name
column.Comment: Name of the query plan step which created this processor. The value is empty if the processor was not added from any step.

column.name: plan_step_description
column.Comment: Description of the query plan step which created this processor. The value is empty if the processor was not added from any step.

column.name: plan_group
column.Comment: Group of the processor if it was created by query plan step. A group is a logical partitioning of processors added from the same query plan step. Group is used only for beautifying the result of EXPLAIN PIPELINE result.

column.name: initial_query_id
column.Comment: ID of the initial query (for distributed query execution).

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: ID of the query.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the processor.

column.name: elapsed_us
column.Comment: Number of microseconds this processor was executed.

column.name: input_wait_elapsed_us
column.Comment: Number of microseconds this processor was waiting for data (from other processor).

column.name: output_wait_elapsed_us
column.Comment: Number of microseconds this processor was waiting because output port was full.

column.name: input_rows
column.Comment: The number of rows consumed by processor.

column.name: input_bytes
column.Comment: The number of bytes consumed by processor.

column.name: output_rows
column.Comment: The number of rows generated by processor.

column.name: output_bytes
column.Comment: The number of bytes generated by processor.

column.name: processor_uniq_id
column.Comment: The uniq processor id in pipeline.

column.name: step_uniq_id
column.Comment: The uniq step id in plan.

column.name: partition
column.Comment: The partition name.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the data part.

column.name: part_type
column.Comment: The data part storing format. Possible Values: Wide (a file per column) and Compact (a single file for all columns).

column.name: parent_name
column.Comment: The name of the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_uuid
column.Comment: The UUID of the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_part_type
column.Comment: The source (parent) data part storing format.

column.name: active
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the data part is active. If a data part is active, it's used in a table. Otherwise, it's about to be deleted. Inactive data parts appear after merging and mutating operations.

column.name: marks
column.Comment: The number of marks. To get the approximate number of rows in a data part, multiply marks by the index granularity (usually 8192) (this hint does not work for adaptive granularity).

column.name: rows
column.Comment: The number of rows.

column.name: bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the data part files in bytes.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks.

column.name: parent_marks
column.Comment: The number of marks in the source (parent) part.

column.name: parent_rows
column.Comment: The number of rows in the source (parent) part.

column.name: parent_bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the source (parent) data part files in bytes.

column.name: parent_data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data in the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks in the source (parent) data part.

column.name: modification_time
column.Comment: The time the directory with the data part was modified. This usually corresponds to the time of data part creation.

column.name: remove_time
column.Comment: The time when the data part became inactive.

column.name: refcount
column.Comment: The number of places where the data part is used. A value greater than 2 indicates that the data part is used in queries or merges.

column.name: min_date
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date key in the data part.

column.name: max_date
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date key in the data part.

column.name: min_time
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date and time key in the data part.

column.name: max_time
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date and time key in the data part.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: ID of the partition.

column.name: min_block_number
column.Comment: The minimum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: max_block_number
column.Comment: The maximum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Depth of the merge tree. Zero means that the current part was created by insert rather than by merging other parts.

column.name: data_version
column.Comment: Number that is used to determine which mutations should be applied to the data part (mutations with a version higher than data_version).

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by primary key values.

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory_allocated
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) reserved for primary key values.

column.name: is_frozen
column.Comment: Flag that shows that a partition data backup exists. 1, the backup exists. 0, the backup does not exist.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Name of the table engine without parameters.

column.name: disk_name
column.Comment: Name of a disk that stores the data part.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Absolute path to the folder with data part files.

column.name: hash_of_all_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of compressed files.

column.name: hash_of_uncompressed_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of uncompressed files (files with marks, index file etc.).

column.name: uncompressed_hash_of_compressed_files
column.Comment: sipHash128 of data in the compressed files as if they were uncompressed.

column.name: delete_ttl_info_min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the date and time key for TTL DELETE rule.

column.name: delete_ttl_info_max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the date and time key for TTL DELETE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: Array of expressions. Each expression defines a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: Array of date and time values. Each element describes the minimum key value for a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: move_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: Array of date and time values. Each element describes the maximum key value for a TTL MOVE rule.

column.name: default_compression_codec
column.Comment: The name of the codec used to compress this data part (in case when there is no explicit codec for columns).

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: recompression_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: group_by_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.expression
column.Comment: The TTL expression.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have at least one row with expired TTL.

column.name: rows_where_ttl_info.max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the calculated TTL expression within this part. Used to understand whether we have all rows with expired TTL.

column.name: is_broken
column.Comment: Whether projection part is broken

column.name: exception_code
column.Comment: Exception message explaining broken state of the projection part

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Exception code explaining broken state of the projection part

column.name: partition
column.Comment: The partition name.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the data part.

column.name: part_type
column.Comment: The data part storing format.

column.name: parent_name
column.Comment: The name of the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_uuid
column.Comment: The UUID of the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_part_type
column.Comment: The source (parent) data part storing format.

column.name: active
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the data part is active

column.name: marks
column.Comment: The number of marks.

column.name: rows
column.Comment: The number of rows.

column.name: bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the data part files in bytes.

column.name: data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data in the data part. All the auxiliary files (for example, files with marks) are not included.

column.name: marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks.

column.name: parent_marks
column.Comment: The number of marks in the source (parent) part.

column.name: parent_rows
column.Comment: The number of rows in the source (parent) part.

column.name: parent_bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of all the source (parent) data part files in bytes.

column.name: parent_data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of uncompressed data in the source (parent) data part.

column.name: parent_marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the file with marks in the source (parent) data part.

column.name: modification_time
column.Comment: The time the directory with the data part was modified. This usually corresponds to the time of data part creation.

column.name: remove_time
column.Comment: The time when the data part became inactive.

column.name: refcount
column.Comment: The number of places where the data part is used. A value greater than 2 indicates that the data part is used in queries or merges.

column.name: min_date
column.Comment: The minimum value for the Date column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: max_date
column.Comment: The maximum value for the Date column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: min_time
column.Comment: The minimum value for the DateTime column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: max_time
column.Comment: The maximum value for the DateTime column if that is included in the partition key.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: ID of the partition.

column.name: min_block_number
column.Comment: The minimum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: max_block_number
column.Comment: The maximum number of data parts that make up the current part after merging.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Depth of the merge tree. Zero means that the current part was created by insert rather than by merging other parts.

column.name: data_version
column.Comment: Number that is used to determine which mutations should be applied to the data part (mutations with a version higher than data_version).

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) used by primary key values.

column.name: primary_key_bytes_in_memory_allocated
column.Comment: The amount of memory (in bytes) reserved for primary key values.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Name of the table engine without parameters.

column.name: disk_name
column.Comment: Name of a disk that stores the data part.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Absolute path to the folder with data part files.

column.name: column
column.Comment: Name of the column.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Column type.

column.name: column_position
column.Comment: Ordinal position of a column in a table starting with 1.

column.name: default_kind
column.Comment: Expression type (DEFAULT, MATERIALIZED, ALIAS) for the default value, or an empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: default_expression
column.Comment: Expression for the default value, or an empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: column_bytes_on_disk
column.Comment: Total size of the column in bytes.

column.name: column_data_compressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of compressed data in the column, in bytes.

column.name: column_data_uncompressed_bytes
column.Comment: Total size of the decompressed data in the column, in bytes.

column.name: column_marks_bytes
column.Comment: The size of the column with marks, in bytes.

column.name: column_modification_time
column.Comment: The last time the column was modified.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Projection name.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Projection type.

column.name: sorting_key
column.Comment: Projection sorting key.

column.name: query
column.Comment: Projection query.

column.name: settings
column.Comment: Projection settings.

column.name: query
column.Comment: Query string.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: ID of the query.

column.name: result_size
column.Comment: Size of the query cache entry.

column.name: tag
column.Comment: Tag of the query cache entry.

column.name: stale
column.Comment: If the query cache entry is stale.

column.name: shared
column.Comment: If the query cache entry is shared between multiple users.

column.name: compressed
column.Comment: If the query cache entry is compressed.

column.name: expires_at
column.Comment: When the query cache entry becomes stale.

column.name: key_hash
column.Comment: A hash of the query string, used as a key to find query cache entries.

column.name: key_hash
column.Comment: Hash of (table_uuid, part_name, condition_hash).

column.name: entry_size
column.Comment: The size of the entry in bytes.

column.name: matching_marks
column.Comment: Matching marks.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Type of an event that occurred when executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: Query starting date.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: Query starting time.

column.name: event_time_microseconds
column.Comment: Query starting time with microseconds precision.

column.name: query_start_time
column.Comment: Start time of query execution.

column.name: query_start_time_microseconds
column.Comment: Start time of query execution with microsecond precision.

column.name: query_duration_ms
column.Comment: Duration of query execution in milliseconds.

column.name: read_rows
column.Comment: Total number of rows read from all tables and table functions participated in query. It includes usual subqueries, subqueries for IN and JOIN. For distributed queries read_rows includes the total number of rows read at all replicas. Each replica sends it's read_rows value, and the server-initiator of the query summarizes all received and local values. The cache volumes do not affect this value.

column.name: read_bytes
column.Comment: Total number of bytes read from all tables and table functions participated in query. It includes usual subqueries, subqueries for IN and JOIN. For distributed queries read_bytes includes the total number of rows read at all replicas. Each replica sends it's read_bytes value, and the server-initiator of the query summarizes all received and local values. The cache volumes do not affect this value.

column.name: written_rows
column.Comment: For INSERT queries, the number of written rows. For other queries, the column value is 0.

column.name: written_bytes
column.Comment: For INSERT queries, the number of written bytes (uncompressed). For other queries, the column value is 0.

column.name: result_rows
column.Comment: Number of rows in a result of the SELECT query, or a number of rows in the INSERT query.

column.name: result_bytes
column.Comment: RAM volume in bytes used to store a query result.

column.name: memory_usage
column.Comment: Memory consumption by the query.

column.name: current_database
column.Comment: Name of the current database.

column.name: query
column.Comment: Query string.

column.name: formatted_query
column.Comment: Formatted query string.

column.name: normalized_query_hash
column.Comment: A numeric hash value, such as it is identical for queries differ only by values of literals.

column.name: query_kind
column.Comment: Type of the query.

column.name: databases
column.Comment: Names of the databases present in the query.

column.name: tables
column.Comment: Names of the tables present in the query.

column.name: columns
column.Comment: Names of the columns present in the query.

column.name: partitions
column.Comment: Names of the partitions present in the query.

column.name: projections
column.Comment: Names of the projections used during the query execution.

column.name: views
column.Comment: Names of the (materialized or live) views present in the query.

column.name: exception_code
column.Comment: Code of an exception.

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Exception message.

column.name: stack_trace
column.Comment: Stack trace. An empty string, if the query was completed successfully.

column.name: is_initial_query
column.Comment: Query type. Possible values: 1 — query was initiated by the client, 0 — query was initiated by another query as part of distributed query execution.

column.name: connection_address
column.Comment: The client IP address from which connection was made.

column.name: connection_port
column.Comment: The client port from which connection was made.

column.name: user
column.Comment: Name of the user who initiated the current query.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: ID of the query.

column.name: address
column.Comment: IP address that was used to make the query.

column.name: port
column.Comment: The client port that was used to make the query.

column.name: initial_user
column.Comment: Name of the user who ran the initial query (for distributed query execution).

column.name: initial_query_id
column.Comment: ID of the initial query (for distributed query execution).

column.name: initial_address
column.Comment: IP address that the parent query was launched from.

column.name: initial_port
column.Comment: The client port that was used to make the parent query.

column.name: initial_query_start_time
column.Comment: Initial query starting time (for distributed query execution).

column.name: initial_query_start_time_microseconds
column.Comment: Initial query starting time with microseconds precision (for distributed query execution).

column.name: authenticated_user
column.Comment: Name of the user who was authenticated in the session.

column.name: interface
column.Comment: Interface that the query was initiated from. Possible values: 1 — TCP, 2 — HTTP.

column.name: is_secure
column.Comment: The flag whether a query was executed over a secure interface

column.name: os_user
column.Comment: Operating system username who runs clickhouse-client.

column.name: client_hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the client machine where the clickhouse-client or another TCP client is run.

column.name: client_name
column.Comment: The clickhouse-client or another TCP client name.

column.name: client_revision
column.Comment: Revision of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client.

column.name: client_version_major
column.Comment: Major version of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client.

column.name: client_version_minor
column.Comment: Minor version of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client.

column.name: client_version_patch
column.Comment: Patch component of the clickhouse-client or another TCP client version.

column.name: script_query_number
column.Comment: The query number in a script with multiple queries for clickhouse-client.

column.name: script_line_number
column.Comment: The line number of the query start in a script with multiple queries for clickhouse-client.

column.name: http_method
column.Comment: HTTP method that initiated the query. Possible values: 0 — The query was launched from the TCP interface, 1 — GET method was used, 2 — POST method was used.

column.name: http_user_agent
column.Comment: HTTP header UserAgent passed in the HTTP query.

column.name: http_referer
column.Comment: HTTP header Referer passed in the HTTP query (contains an absolute or partial address of the page making the query).

column.name: forwarded_for
column.Comment: HTTP header X-Forwarded-For passed in the HTTP query.

column.name: quota_key
column.Comment: The quota key specified in the quotas setting (see keyed).

column.name: distributed_depth
column.Comment: How many times a query was forwarded between servers.

column.name: revision
