column.Comment: ClickHouse revision.

column.name: log_comment
column.Comment: Log comment. It can be set to arbitrary string no longer than max_query_size. An empty string if it is not defined.

column.name: thread_ids
column.Comment: Thread ids that are participating in query execution. These threads may not have run simultaneously.

column.name: peak_threads_usage
column.Comment: Maximum count of simultaneous threads executing the query.

column.name: ProfileEvents
column.Comment: ProfileEvents that measure different metrics. The description of them could be found in the table system.events

column.name: Settings
column.Comment: Settings that were changed when the client ran the query. To enable logging changes to settings, set the log_query_settings parameter to 1.

column.name: used_aggregate_functions
column.Comment: Canonical names of aggregate functions, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_aggregate_function_combinators
column.Comment: Canonical names of aggregate functions combinators, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_database_engines
column.Comment: Canonical names of database engines, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_data_type_families
column.Comment: Canonical names of data type families, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_dictionaries
column.Comment: Canonical names of dictionaries, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_formats
column.Comment: Canonical names of formats, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_functions
column.Comment: Canonical names of functions, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_storages
column.Comment: Canonical names of storages, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_table_functions
column.Comment: Canonical names of table functions, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_executable_user_defined_functions
column.Comment: Canonical names of executable user defined functions, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_sql_user_defined_functions
column.Comment: Canonical names of sql user defined functions, which were used during query execution.

column.name: used_row_policies
column.Comment: The list of row policies names that were used during query execution.

column.name: used_privileges
column.Comment: Privileges which were successfully checked during query execution.

column.name: missing_privileges
column.Comment: Privileges that are missing during query execution.

column.name: transaction_id
column.Comment: The identifier of the transaction in scope of which this query was executed.

column.name: query_cache_usage
column.Comment: Usage of the query cache during query execution. Values: 'Unknown' = Status unknown, 'None' = The query result was neither written into nor read from the query result cache, 'Write' = The query result was written into the query result cache, 'Read' = The query result was read from the query result cache.

column.name: asynchronous_read_counters
column.Comment: Metrics for asynchronous reading.

column.name: is_internal
column.Comment: Indicates whether it is an auxiliary query executed internally.

column.name: quota_name
column.Comment: Quota name.

column.name: duration
column.Comment: Length of the time interval for calculating resource consumption, in seconds.

column.name: is_randomized_interval
column.Comment: Boolean value. It shows whether the interval is randomized. Interval always starts at the same time if it is not randomized. For example, an interval of 1 minute always starts at an integer number of minutes (i.e. it can start at 11:20:00, but it never starts at 11:20:01), an interval of one day always starts at midnight UTC. If interval is randomized, the very first interval starts at random time, and subsequent intervals starts one by one. Values: 0 — Interval is not randomized, 1 — Interval is randomized.

column.name: max_queries
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of queries of all types allowed to be executed.

column.name: max_query_selects
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of SELECT queries allowed to be executed.

column.name: max_query_inserts
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of INSERT queries allowed to be executed.

column.name: max_errors
column.Comment: The maximum number of queries resulted in an error allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: max_result_rows
column.Comment: The maximum total number of rows in the result set of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: max_result_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum total number of bytes in the result set of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: max_read_rows
column.Comment: The maximum number of rows to read during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: max_read_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum number of bytes to read during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: max_execution_time
column.Comment: The maximum amount of time (in nanoseconds) allowed for all queries to execute within the specified period of time

column.name: max_written_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum number of bytes to written during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: max_failed_sequential_authentications
column.Comment: The maximum number of consecutive authentication failures allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: quota_name
column.Comment: Quota name.

column.name: quota_key
column.Comment: Key value.

column.name: start_time
column.Comment: Start time for calculating resource consumption.

column.name: end_time
column.Comment: End time for calculating resource consumption.

column.name: duration
column.Comment: Length of the time interval for calculating resource consumption, in seconds.

column.name: queries
column.Comment: The current number of executed queries.

column.name: max_queries
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of queries of all types allowed to be executed.

column.name: query_selects
column.Comment: The current number of executed SELECT queries.

column.name: max_query_selects
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of SELECT queries allowed to be executed.

column.name: query_inserts
column.Comment: The current number of executed INSERT queries.

column.name: max_query_inserts
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of INSERT queries allowed to be executed.

column.name: errors
column.Comment: The current number of queries resulted in an error.

column.name: max_errors
column.Comment: The maximum number of queries resulted in an error allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: result_rows
column.Comment: The current total number of rows in the result set of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_result_rows
column.Comment: The maximum total number of rows in the result set of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: result_bytes
column.Comment: The current total number of bytes in the result set of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_result_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum total number of bytes in the result set of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: read_rows
column.Comment: The current total number of rows read during execution of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_read_rows
column.Comment: The maximum number of rows to read during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: read_bytes
column.Comment: The current total number of bytes read during execution of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_read_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum number of bytes to read during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: execution_time
column.Comment: The current total amount of time (in nanoseconds) spent to execute queries within the current period of time

column.name: max_execution_time
column.Comment: The maximum amount of time (in nanoseconds) allowed for all queries to execute within the specified period of time

column.name: written_bytes
column.Comment: The current total number of bytes written during execution of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_written_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum number of bytes to written during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: failed_sequential_authentications
column.Comment: The current number of consecutive authentication failures within the current period of time.

column.name: max_failed_sequential_authentications
column.Comment: The maximum number of consecutive authentication failures allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Quota name.

column.name: id
column.Comment: Quota ID.

column.name: storage
column.Comment: Storage of quotas. Possible value: 'users.xml' if a quota configured in the users.xml file, 'disk' if a quota configured by an SQL-query.

column.name: keys
column.Comment: Key specifies how the quota should be shared. If two connections use the same quota and key, they share the same amounts of resources. Values: [] — All users share the same quota, ['user_name'] — Connections with the same user name share the same quota, ['ip_address'] — Connections from the same IP share the same quota. ['client_key'] — Connections with the same key share the same quota. A key must be explicitly provided by a client. When using clickhouse-client, pass a key value in the --quota_key parameter, or use the quota_key parameter in the client configuration file. When using HTTP interface, use the X-ClickHouse-Quota header, ['user_name', 'client_key'] — Connections with the same client_key share the same quota. If a key isn't provided by a client, the quota is tracked for `user_name`, ['client_key', 'ip_address'] — Connections with the same client_key share the same quota. If a key isn't provided by a client, the quota is tracked for ip_address.

column.name: durations
column.Comment: Time interval lengths in seconds.

column.name: apply_to_all
column.Comment: Logical value. It shows which users the quota is applied to. Values: 0 — The quota applies to users specify in the apply_to_list. 1 — The quota applies to all users except those listed in apply_to_except.

column.name: apply_to_list
column.Comment: List of user names/roles that the quota should be applied to.

column.name: apply_to_except
column.Comment: List of user names/roles that the quota should not apply to.

column.name: quota_name
column.Comment: Quota name.

column.name: quota_key
column.Comment: Key value.

column.name: is_current
column.Comment: Quota usage for current user.

column.name: start_time
column.Comment: Start time for calculating resource consumption.

column.name: end_time
column.Comment: End time for calculating resource consumption.

column.name: duration
column.Comment: Length of the time interval for calculating resource consumption, in seconds.

column.name: queries
column.Comment: The current number of executed queries.

column.name: max_queries
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of queries of all types allowed to be executed.

column.name: query_selects
column.Comment: The current number of executed SELECT queries.

column.name: max_query_selects
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of SELECT queries allowed to be executed.

column.name: query_inserts
column.Comment: The current number of executed INSERT queries.

column.name: max_query_inserts
column.Comment: The maximum allowed number of INSERT queries allowed to be executed.

column.name: errors
column.Comment: The current number of queries resulted in an error.

column.name: max_errors
column.Comment: The maximum number of queries resulted in an error allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: result_rows
column.Comment: The current total number of rows in the result set of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_result_rows
column.Comment: The maximum total number of rows in the result set of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: result_bytes
column.Comment: The current total number of bytes in the result set of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_result_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum total number of bytes in the result set of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: read_rows
column.Comment: The current total number of rows read during execution of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_read_rows
column.Comment: The maximum number of rows to read during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: read_bytes
column.Comment: The current total number of bytes read during execution of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_read_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum number of bytes to read during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: execution_time
column.Comment: The current total amount of time (in nanoseconds) spent to execute queries within the current period of time

column.name: max_execution_time
column.Comment: The maximum amount of time (in nanoseconds) allowed for all queries to execute within the specified period of time

column.name: written_bytes
column.Comment: The current total number of bytes written during execution of all queries within the current period of time.

column.name: max_written_bytes
column.Comment: The maximum number of bytes to written during execution of all queries allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: failed_sequential_authentications
column.Comment: The current number of consecutive authentication failures within the current period of time.

column.name: max_failed_sequential_authentications
column.Comment: The maximum number of consecutive authentication failures allowed within the specified period of time.

column.name: disk_name
column.Comment: Disk name.

column.name: path
column.Comment: Disk path.

column.name: cache_base_path
column.Comment: Base directory of cache files.

column.name: local_path
column.Comment: Path of ClickHouse file, also used as metadata path.

column.name: remote_path
column.Comment: Blob path in object storage, with which ClickHouse file is associated with.

column.name: size
column.Comment: Size of the file (compressed).

column.name: common_prefix_for_blobs
column.Comment: Common prefix for blobs in object storage.

column.name: cache_paths
column.Comment: Cache files for corresponding blob.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: Table UUID.

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Table engine name.

column.name: is_leader
column.Comment: Whether the replica is the leader. Multiple replicas can be leaders at the same time. A replica can be prevented from becoming a leader using the merge_tree setting replicated_can_become_leader. The leaders are responsible for scheduling background merges. Note that writes can be performed to any replica that is available and has a session in ZK, regardless of whether it is a leader.

column.name: can_become_leader
column.Comment: Whether the replica can be a leader.

column.name: is_readonly
column.Comment: Whether the replica is in read-only mode. This mode is turned on if the config does not have sections with ClickHouse Keeper, if an unknown error occurred when reinitializing sessions in ClickHouse Keeper, and during session reinitialization in ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: readonly_start_time
column.Comment: The timestamp when the replica transitioned into readonly mode. Null if the replica is not in readonly mode.

column.name: is_session_expired
column.Comment: Whether the session with ClickHouse Keeper has expired. Basically the same as `is_readonly`.

column.name: future_parts
column.Comment: The number of data parts that will appear as the result of INSERTs or merges that haven't been done yet.

column.name: parts_to_check
column.Comment: The number of data parts in the queue for verification. A part is put in the verification queue if there is suspicion that it might be damaged.

column.name: zookeeper_name
column.Comment: The name of the the [Zoo]Keeper cluster (possibly auxiliary one) where the table's metadata is stored

column.name: zookeeper_path
column.Comment: Path to table data in ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: replica_name
column.Comment: Replica name in ClickHouse Keeper. Different replicas of the same table have different names.

column.name: replica_path
column.Comment: Path to replica data in ClickHouse Keeper. The same as concatenating 'zookeeper_path/replicas/replica_path'.

column.name: columns_version
column.Comment: Version number of the table structure. Indicates how many times ALTER was performed. If replicas have different versions, it means some replicas haven't made all of the ALTERs yet.

column.name: queue_size
column.Comment: Size of the queue for operations waiting to be performed. Operations include inserting blocks of data, merges, and certain other actions. It usually coincides with future_parts.

column.name: inserts_in_queue
column.Comment: Number of inserts of blocks of data that need to be made. Insertions are usually replicated fairly quickly. If this number is large, it means something is wrong.

column.name: merges_in_queue
column.Comment: The number of merges waiting to be made. Sometimes merges are lengthy, so this value may be greater than zero for a long time.

column.name: part_mutations_in_queue
column.Comment: The number of mutations waiting to be made.

column.name: queue_oldest_time
column.Comment: If `queue_size` greater than 0, shows when the oldest operation was added to the queue.

column.name: inserts_oldest_time
column.Comment: See `queue_oldest_time`.

column.name: merges_oldest_time
column.Comment: See `queue_oldest_time`.

column.name: part_mutations_oldest_time
column.Comment: See `queue_oldest_time`.

column.name: oldest_part_to_get
column.Comment: The name of the part to fetch from other replicas obtained from the oldest GET_PARTS entry in the replication queue.

column.name: oldest_part_to_merge_to
column.Comment: The result part name to merge to obtained from the oldest MERGE_PARTS entry in the replication queue.

column.name: oldest_part_to_mutate_to
column.Comment: The result part name to mutate to obtained from the oldest MUTATE_PARTS entry in the replication queue.

column.name: log_max_index
column.Comment: Maximum entry number in the log of general activity.

column.name: log_pointer
column.Comment: Maximum entry number in the log of general activity that the replica copied to its execution queue, plus one. If log_pointer is much smaller than log_max_index, something is wrong.

column.name: last_queue_update
column.Comment: When the queue was updated last time.

column.name: absolute_delay
column.Comment: How big lag in seconds the current replica has.

column.name: total_replicas
column.Comment: The total number of known replicas of this table.

column.name: active_replicas
column.Comment: The number of replicas of this table that have a session in ClickHouse Keeper (i.e., the number of functioning replicas).

column.name: lost_part_count
column.Comment: The number of data parts lost in the table by all replicas in total since table creation. Value is persisted in ClickHouse Keeper and can only increase.

column.name: last_queue_update_exception
column.Comment: When the queue contains broken entries. Especially important when ClickHouse breaks backward compatibility between versions and log entries written by newer versions aren't parseable by old versions.

column.name: zookeeper_exception
column.Comment: The last exception message, got if the error happened when fetching the info from ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: replica_is_active
column.Comment: Map between replica name and is replica active.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: elapsed
column.Comment: The time elapsed (in seconds) since showing currently running background fetches started.

column.name: progress
column.Comment: The percentage of completed work from 0 to 1.

column.name: result_part_name
column.Comment: The name of the part that will be formed as the result of showing currently running background fetches.

column.name: result_part_path
column.Comment: Absolute path to the part that will be formed as the result of showing currently running background fetches.

column.name: partition_id
column.Comment: ID of the partition.

column.name: total_size_bytes_compressed
column.Comment: The total size (in bytes) of the compressed data in the result part.

column.name: bytes_read_compressed
column.Comment: The number of compressed bytes read from the result part.

column.name: source_replica_path
column.Comment: Absolute path to the source replica.

column.name: source_replica_hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the source replica.

column.name: source_replica_port
column.Comment: Port number of the source replica.

column.name: interserver_scheme
column.Comment: Name of the interserver scheme.

column.name: URI
column.Comment: Uniform resource identifier.

column.name: to_detached
column.Comment: The flag indicates whether the currently running background fetch is being performed using the TO DETACHED expression.

column.name: thread_id
column.Comment: Thread identifier.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Setting name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Setting value.

column.name: default
column.Comment: Setting default value.

column.name: changed
column.Comment: 1 if the setting was explicitly defined in the config or explicitly changed.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Setting description.

column.name: min
column.Comment: Minimum value of the setting, if any is set via constraints. If the setting has no minimum value, contains NULL.

column.name: max
column.Comment: Maximum value of the setting, if any is set via constraints. If the setting has no maximum value, contains NULL.

column.name: disallowed_values
column.Comment: List of disallowed values

column.name: readonly
column.Comment: Shows whether the current user can change the setting: 0 — Current user can change the setting, 1 — Current user can't change the setting.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Setting type (implementation specific string value).

column.name: is_obsolete
column.Comment: Shows whether a setting is obsolete.

column.name: tier
column.Comment: Support level for this feature. ClickHouse features are organized in tiers, varying depending on the current status of their development and the expectations one might have when using them: * PRODUCTION: The feature is stable, safe to use and does not have issues interacting with other PRODUCTION features. * BETA: The feature is stable and safe. The outcome of using it together with other features is unknown and correctness is not guaranteed. Testing and reports are welcome. * EXPERIMENTAL: The feature is under development. Only intended for developers and ClickHouse enthusiasts. The feature might or might not work and could be removed at any time. * OBSOLETE: No longer supported. Either it is already removed or it will be removed in future releases.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Name of the database.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table.

column.name: replica_name
column.Comment: Replica name in ClickHouse Keeper. Different replicas of the same table have different names.

column.name: position
column.Comment: Position of the task in the queue.

column.name: node_name
column.Comment: Node name in ClickHouse Keeper.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Type of the task in the queue, one of: • GET_PART — Get the part from another replica, • ATTACH_PART — Attach the part, possibly from our own replica (if found in the detached folder). You may think of it as a GET_PART with some optimizations as they're nearly identical, • MERGE_PARTS — Merge the parts, • DROP_RANGE — Delete the parts in the specified partition in the specified number range. • CLEAR_COLUMN — NOTE: Deprecated. Drop specific column from specified partition. • CLEAR_INDEX — NOTE: Deprecated. Drop specific index from specified partition. • REPLACE_RANGE — Drop a certain range of parts and replace them with new ones. • MUTATE_PART — Apply one or several mutations to the part. • ALTER_METADATA — Apply alter modification according to global /metadata and /columns paths.

column.name: create_time
column.Comment: Date and time when the task was submitted for execution.

column.name: required_quorum
column.Comment: The number of replicas waiting for the task to complete with confirmation of completion. This column is only relevant for the GET_PARTS task.

column.name: source_replica
column.Comment: Name of the source replica.

column.name: new_part_name
column.Comment: Name of the new part.

column.name: parts_to_merge
column.Comment: Names of parts to merge or update.

column.name: is_detach
column.Comment: The flag indicates whether the DETACH_PARTS task is in the queue.

column.name: is_currently_executing
column.Comment: The flag indicates whether a specific task is being performed right now.

column.name: num_tries
column.Comment: The number of failed attempts to complete the task.

column.name: last_exception
column.Comment: Text message about the last error that occurred (if any).

column.name: last_exception_time
column.Comment: Date and time when the last error occurred.

column.name: last_attempt_time
column.Comment: Date and time when the task was last attempted.

column.name: num_postponed
column.Comment: The number of times the action was postponed.

column.name: postpone_reason
column.Comment: The reason why the task was postponed.

column.name: last_postpone_time
column.Comment: Date and time when the task was last postponed.

column.name: merge_type
column.Comment: Type of the current merge. Empty if it's a mutation.

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of the resource.

column.name: read_disks
column.Comment: The list of disk names that uses this resource for read operations.

column.name: write_disks
column.Comment: The list of disk names that uses this resource for write operations.

column.name: unit
column.Comment: Resource unit used for cost measurements.

column.name: create_query
column.Comment: CREATE query of the resource.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table with StorageEmbeddedRocksDB engine.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Metric name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Metric value.

column.name: user_name
column.Comment: User name.

column.name: role_name
column.Comment: Role name.

column.name: granted_role_name
column.Comment: Name of role granted to the `role_name` role. To grant one role to another one use `GRANT role1 TO role2`.

column.name: granted_role_id
column.Comment: The ID of the role.

column.name: granted_role_is_default
column.Comment: Flag that shows whether `granted_role` is a default role. Possible values: • 1 — `granted_role` is a default role, • 0 — `granted_role` is not a default role.

column.name: with_admin_option
column.Comment: Flag that shows whether `granted_role` is a role with `ADMIN OPTION` privilege. Possible values: • 1 — The role has `ADMIN OPTION` privilege.• 0 — The role without `ADMIN OPTION` privilege.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Role name.

column.name: id
column.Comment: Role ID.

column.name: storage
column.Comment: Path to the storage of roles. Configured in the `access_control_path` parameter.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of a row policy.

column.name: short_name
column.Comment: Short name of a row policy. Names of row policies are compound, for example: myfilter ON mydb.mytable. Here 'myfilter ON mydb.mytable' is the name of the row policy, 'myfilter' is it's short name.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database name.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Table name. Empty if policy for database.

column.name: id
column.Comment: Row policy ID.

column.name: storage
column.Comment: Name of the directory where the row policy is stored.

column.name: select_filter
column.Comment: Expression which is used for filtering in SELECT queries.

column.name: is_restrictive
column.Comment: Shows whether the row policy restricts access to rows. Value: • 0 — The row policy is defined with `AS PERMISSIVE` clause, • 1 — The row policy is defined with AS RESTRICTIVE clause.

column.name: apply_to_all
column.Comment: Shows that the row policies set for all roles and/or users.

column.name: apply_to_list
column.Comment: List of the roles and/or users to which the row policies is applied.

column.name: apply_to_except
column.Comment: The row policies is applied to all roles and/or users excepting of the listed ones.

column.name: database
column.Comment: Database of the table with S3Queue Engine.

column.name: table
column.Comment: Name of the table with S3Queue Engine.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Setting name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Setting value.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Setting type (implementation specific string value).

column.name: changed
column.Comment: 1 if the setting was explicitly defined in the config or explicitly changed.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Setting description.

column.name: alterable
column.Comment: Shows whether the current user can change the setting via ALTER TABLE MODIFY SETTING: 0 — Current user can change the setting, 1 — Current user can't change the setting.

column.name: zookeeper_path
column.Comment: Path in zookeeper to metadata

column.name: file_path
column.Comment: File path of a file which is being processed

column.name: file_name
column.Comment: File name of a file which is being processed

column.name: rows_processed
column.Comment: Currently processed number of rows

column.name: status
column.Comment: Status of processing: Processed, Processing, Failed

column.name: processing_start_time
column.Comment: Time at which processing of the file started

column.name: processing_end_time
column.Comment: Time at which processing of the file ended

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Exception which happened during processing

column.name: resource
column.Comment: Resource name

column.name: path
column.Comment: Path to a scheduling node within this resource scheduling hierarchy

column.name: type
column.Comment: Type of a scheduling node.

column.name: weight
column.Comment: Weight of a node, used by a parent node of `fair` type.

column.name: priority
column.Comment: Priority of a node, used by a parent node of 'priority' type (Lower value means higher priority).

column.name: is_active
column.Comment: Whether this node is currently active - has resource requests to be dequeued and constraints satisfied.

column.name: active_children
column.Comment: The number of children in active state.

column.name: dequeued_requests
column.Comment: The total number of resource requests dequeued from this node.

column.name: canceled_requests
column.Comment: The total number of resource requests canceled from this node.

column.name: dequeued_cost
column.Comment: The sum of costs (e.g. size in bytes) of all requests dequeued from this node.

column.name: throughput
column.Comment: Current average throughput (dequeued cost per second).

column.name: canceled_cost
column.Comment: The sum of costs (e.g. size in bytes) of all requests canceled from this node.

column.name: busy_periods
column.Comment: The total number of deactivations of this node.

column.name: vruntime
column.Comment: For children of `fair` nodes only. Virtual runtime of a node used by SFQ algorithm to select the next child to process in a max-min fair manner.

column.name: system_vruntime
column.Comment: For `fair` nodes only. Virtual runtime showing `vruntime` of the last processed resource request. Used during child activation as the new value of `vruntime`.

column.name: queue_length
column.Comment: For `fifo` nodes only. Current number of resource requests residing in the queue.

column.name: queue_cost
column.Comment: For fifo nodes only. Sum of costs (e.g. size in bytes) of all requests residing in the queue.

column.name: budget
column.Comment: For fifo nodes only. The number of available 'cost units' for new resource requests. Can appear in case of discrepancy of estimated and real costs of resource requests (e.g. after read/write failure)

column.name: is_satisfied
column.Comment: For constraint nodes only (e.g. `inflight_limit`). Equals to `1` if all the constraint of this node are satisfied.

column.name: inflight_requests
column.Comment: For `inflight_limit` nodes only. The number of resource requests dequeued from this node, that are currently in consumption state.

column.name: inflight_cost
column.Comment: For `inflight_limit` nodes only. The sum of costs (e.g. bytes) of all resource requests dequeued from this node, that are currently in consumption state.

column.name: max_requests
column.Comment: For `inflight_limit` nodes only. Upper limit for inflight_requests leading to constraint violation.

column.name: max_cost
column.Comment: For `inflight_limit` nodes only. Upper limit for inflight_cost leading to constraint violation.

column.name: max_speed
column.Comment: For `bandwidth_limit` nodes only. Upper limit for bandwidth in tokens per second.

column.name: max_burst
column.Comment: For `bandwidth_limit` nodes only. Upper limit for tokens available in token-bucket throttler.

column.name: throttling_us
column.Comment: For `bandwidth_limit` nodes only. Total number of microseconds this node was in throttling state.

column.name: tokens
column.Comment: For `bandwidth_limit` nodes only. Number of tokens currently available in token-bucket throttler.

column.name: storage
column.Comment: Storage name: File, URL, S3 or HDFS.

column.name: source
column.Comment: File source.

column.name: format
column.Comment: Format name.

column.name: additional_format_info
column.Comment: Additional information required to identify the schema. For example, format specific settings.

column.name: registration_time
column.Comment: Timestamp when schema was added in cache.

column.name: schema
column.Comment: Cached schema.

column.name: number_of_rows
column.Comment: Number of rows in the file in given format. It's used for caching trivial count() from data files and for caching number of rows from the metadata during schema inference.

column.name: schema_inference_mode
column.Comment: Scheme inference mode.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Server setting name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Server setting value.

column.name: default
column.Comment: Server setting default value.

column.name: changed
column.Comment: Shows whether a setting was specified in config.xml

column.name: description
column.Comment: Short server setting description.

column.name: type
column.Comment: Server setting value type.

column.name: changeable_without_restart
column.Comment: Shows whether a setting can be changed at runtime.

column.name: is_obsolete
column.Comment: Shows whether a setting is obsolete.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Setting name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Setting value.

column.name: changed
column.Comment: Shows whether the setting was explicitly defined in the config or explicitly changed.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Short setting description.

column.name: min
column.Comment: Minimum value of the setting, if any is set via constraints. If the setting has no minimum value, contains NULL.

column.name: max
column.Comment: Maximum value of the setting, if any is set via constraints. If the setting has no maximum value, contains NULL.

column.name: disallowed_values
column.Comment: List of disallowed values

column.name: readonly
column.Comment: Shows whether the current user can change the setting: 0 — Current user can change the setting, 1 — Current user can't change the setting.

column.name: type
column.Comment: The type of the value that can be assigned to this setting.

column.name: default
column.Comment: Setting default value.

column.name: alias_for
column.Comment: The name of the original setting if the setting is an alias for another setting.

column.name: is_obsolete
column.Comment: Shows whether a setting is obsolete.

column.name: tier
column.Comment: Support level for this feature. ClickHouse features are organized in tiers, varying depending on the current status of their development and the expectations one might have when using them: * PRODUCTION: The feature is stable, safe to use and does not have issues interacting with other PRODUCTION features. * BETA: The feature is stable and safe. The outcome of using it together with other features is unknown and correctness is not guaranteed. Testing and reports are welcome. * EXPERIMENTAL: The feature is under development. Only intended for developers and ClickHouse enthusiasts. The feature might or might not work and could be removed at any time. * OBSOLETE: No longer supported. Either it is already removed or it will be removed in future releases.

column.name: type
column.Comment: The group of settings (Session, MergeTree...)

column.name: version
column.Comment: The ClickHouse server version.

column.name: changes
column.Comment: The list of changes in settings which changed the behaviour of ClickHouse.
column.StoredType: Array(Tuple(name String, previous_value String, new_value String, reason String))|0v

column.name: profile_name
column.Comment: Setting profile name.

column.name: user_name
column.Comment: User name.

column.name: role_name
column.Comment: Role name.

column.name: index
column.Comment: Sequential number of the settings profile element.

column.name: setting_name
column.Comment: Setting name.

column.name: value
column.Comment: Setting value.

column.name: min
column.Comment: The minimum value of the setting. NULL if not set.

column.name: max
column.Comment: The maximum value of the setting. NULL if not set.

column.name: writability
column.Comment: The property which shows whether a setting can be changed or not.

column.name: inherit_profile
column.Comment: A parent profile for this setting profile. NULL if not set. Setting profile will inherit all the settings' values and constraints (min, max, readonly) from its parent profiles.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Setting profile name.

column.name: id
column.Comment: Setting profile ID.

column.name: storage
column.Comment: Path to the storage of setting profiles. Configured in the `access_control_path` parameter.

column.name: num_elements
column.Comment: Number of elements for this profile in the `system.settings_profile_elements` table.

column.name: apply_to_all
column.Comment: Shows that the settings profile set for all roles and/or users.

column.name: apply_to_list
column.Comment: List of the roles and/or users to which the setting profile is applied.

column.name: apply_to_except
column.Comment: The setting profile is applied to all roles and/or users excepting of the listed ones.

column.name: thread_name
column.Comment: The name of the thread.

column.name: thread_id
column.Comment: The thread identifier

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: The ID of the query this thread belongs to.

column.name: trace
column.Comment: The stacktrace of this thread. Basically just an array of addresses.

column.name: policy_name
column.Comment: The name of the storage policy.

column.name: volume_name
column.Comment: The name of the volume.

column.name: volume_priority
column.Comment: The priority of the volume.

column.name: disks
column.Comment: The list of all disks names which are a part of this storage policy.

column.name: volume_type
column.Comment: The type of the volume - JBOD or a single disk.

column.name: max_data_part_size
column.Comment: the maximum size of a part that can be stored on any of the volumes disks.

column.name: move_factor
column.Comment: When the amount of available space gets lower than this factor, data automatically starts to move on the next volume if any (by default, 0.1).

column.name: prefer_not_to_merge
column.Comment: You should not use this setting. Disables merging of data parts on this volume (this is harmful and leads to performance degradation).

column.name: perform_ttl_move_on_insert
column.Comment: Disables TTL move on data part INSERT. By default (if enabled) if we insert a data part that already expired by the TTL move rule it immediately goes to a volume/disk declared in move rule.

column.name: load_balancing
column.Comment: Policy for disk balancing, `round_robin` or `least_used`.

column.name: symbol
column.Comment: Symbol name in the binary. It is mangled. You can apply demangle(symbol) to obtain a readable name.

column.name: symbol_demangled
column.Comment: Demangled symbol used for XRay instrumentation.

column.name: function_id
column.Comment: Function ID in the XRay instrumentation map.

column.name: address_begin
column.Comment: Start address of the symbol in the binary.

column.name: address_end
column.Comment: End address of the symbol in the binary.

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of table engine.

column.name: supports_settings
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if table engine supports SETTINGS clause.

column.name: supports_skipping_indices
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if table engine supports skipping indices.

column.name: supports_projections
column.Comment: Flag that indicated if table engine supports projections.

column.name: supports_sort_order
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if table engine supports clauses PARTITION_BY, PRIMARY_KEY, ORDER_BY and SAMPLE_BY.

column.name: supports_ttl
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if table engine supports TTL.

column.name: supports_replication
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if table engine supports data replication.

column.name: supports_deduplication
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if table engine supports data deduplication.

column.name: supports_parallel_insert
column.Comment: Flag that indicates if table engine supports parallel insert (see max_insert_threads setting).

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of a table function.

column.name: description
column.Comment: Brief description of a table function.

column.name: allow_readonly
column.Comment: Flag that indicated whether a readonly user may use this function.

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the database the table is in.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: Table uuid (Atomic database).

column.name: engine
column.Comment: Table engine name (without parameters).

column.name: is_temporary
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the table is temporary.

column.name: data_paths
column.Comment: Paths to the table data in the file systems.

column.name: metadata_path
column.Comment: Path to the table metadata in the file system.

column.name: metadata_modification_time
column.Comment: Time of latest modification of the table metadata.

column.name: metadata_version
column.Comment: Metadata version for ReplicatedMergeTree table, 0 for non ReplicatedMergeTree table.

column.name: dependencies_database
column.Comment: Database dependencies.

column.name: dependencies_table
column.Comment: Table dependencies (materialized views the current table).

column.name: create_table_query
column.Comment: The query that was used to create the table.

column.name: engine_full
column.Comment: Parameters of the table engine.

column.name: as_select
column.Comment: SELECT query for view.

column.name: parameterized_view_parameters
column.Comment: Parameters of parameterized view.

column.name: partition_key
column.Comment: The partition key expression specified in the table.

column.name: sorting_key
column.Comment: The sorting key expression specified in the table.

column.name: primary_key
column.Comment: The primary key expression specified in the table.

column.name: sampling_key
column.Comment: The sampling key expression specified in the table.

column.name: storage_policy
column.Comment: The storage policy.

column.name: total_rows
column.Comment: Total number of rows, if it is possible to quickly determine exact number of rows in the table, otherwise NULL (including underlying Buffer table).

column.name: total_bytes
column.Comment: Total number of bytes, if it is possible to quickly determine exact number of bytes for the table on storage, otherwise NULL (does not includes any underlying storage). If the table stores data on disk, returns used space on disk (i.e. compressed). If the table stores data in memory, returns approximated number of used bytes in memory.

column.name: total_bytes_uncompressed
column.Comment: Total number of uncompressed bytes, if it's possible to quickly determine the exact number of bytes from the part checksums for the table on storage, otherwise NULL (does not take underlying storage (if any) into account).

column.name: parts
column.Comment: The total number of parts in this table.

column.name: active_parts
column.Comment: The number of active parts in this table.

column.name: total_marks
column.Comment: The total number of marks in all parts in this table.

column.name: active_on_fly_data_mutations
column.Comment: Total number of active data mutations (UPDATEs and DELETEs) suitable for applying on the fly.

column.name: active_on_fly_alter_mutations
column.Comment: Total number of active alter mutations (MODIFY COLUMNs) suitable for applying on the fly.

column.name: active_on_fly_metadata_mutations
column.Comment: Total number of active metadata mutations (RENAMEs) suitable for applying on the fly.

column.name: columns_descriptions_cache_size
column.Comment: Size of columns description cache for *MergeTree tables

column.name: lifetime_rows
column.Comment: Total number of rows INSERTed since server start (only for Buffer tables).

column.name: lifetime_bytes
column.Comment: Total number of bytes INSERTed since server start (only for Buffer tables).

column.name: comment
column.Comment: The comment for the table.

column.name: has_own_data
column.Comment: Flag that indicates whether the table itself stores some data on disk or only accesses some other source.

column.name: loading_dependencies_database
column.Comment: Database loading dependencies (list of objects which should be loaded before the current object).

column.name: loading_dependencies_table
column.Comment: Table loading dependencies (list of objects which should be loaded before the current object).

column.name: loading_dependent_database
column.Comment: Dependent loading database.

column.name: loading_dependent_table
column.Comment: Dependent loading table.

column.name: definer
column.Comment: SQL security definer's name used for the table.

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: Date of the entry.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: Time of the entry.

column.name: event_time_microseconds
column.Comment: Time of the entry with microseconds precision.

column.name: thread_name
column.Comment: Name of the thread from which the logging was done.

column.name: thread_id
column.Comment: OS thread ID.

column.name: level
column.Comment: Entry level. Possible values: 1 or 'Fatal', 2 or 'Critical', 3 or 'Error', 4 or 'Warning', 5 or 'Notice', 6 or 'Information', 7 or 'Debug', 8 or 'Trace'.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: ID of the query.

column.name: logger_name
column.Comment: Name of the logger (e.g., DDLWorker).

column.name: message
column.Comment: The message itself.

column.name: revision
column.Comment: ClickHouse revision.

column.name: source_file
column.Comment: Source file from which the logging was done.

column.name: source_line
column.Comment: Source line from which the logging was done.

column.name: message_format_string
column.Comment: A format string that was used to format the message.

column.name: value1
column.Comment: Argument 1 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value2
column.Comment: Argument 2 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value3
column.Comment: Argument 3 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value4
column.Comment: Argument 4 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value5
column.Comment: Argument 5 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value6
column.Comment: Argument 6 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value7
column.Comment: Argument 7 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value8
column.Comment: Argument 8 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value9
column.Comment: Argument 9 that was used to format the message.

column.name: value10
column.Comment: Argument 10 that was used to format the message.

column.name: time_zone
column.Comment: List of supported time zones.

column.name: name
column.Comment: Name of the tokenizer

column.name: hostname
column.Comment: Hostname of the server executing the query.

column.name: event_date
column.Comment: Date of sampling moment.

column.name: event_time
column.Comment: Timestamp of the sampling moment.

column.name: event_time_microseconds
column.Comment: Timestamp of the sampling moment with microseconds precision.

column.name: timestamp_ns
column.Comment: Timestamp of the sampling moment in nanoseconds.

column.name: revision
column.Comment: ClickHouse server build revision.

column.name: trace_type
column.Comment: Trace type: `Real` represents collecting stack traces by wall-clock time. `CPU` represents collecting stack traces by CPU time. `Memory` represents collecting allocations and deallocations when memory allocation exceeds the subsequent watermark. `MemorySample` represents collecting random allocations and deallocations. `MemoryPeak` represents collecting updates of peak memory usage. `ProfileEvent` represents collecting of increments of profile events. `JemallocSample` represents collecting of jemalloc samples. `MemoryAllocatedWithoutCheck` represents collection of significant allocations (>16MiB) that is done with ignoring any memory limits (for ClickHouse developers only).`Instrumentation` represents traces collected by the instrumentation performed through XRay.

column.name: cpu_id
column.Comment: CPU identifier.

column.name: thread_id
column.Comment: Thread identifier.

column.name: thread_name
column.Comment: Thread name.

column.name: query_id
column.Comment: Query identifier that can be used to get details about a query that was running from the query_log system table.

column.name: trace
column.Comment: Stack trace at the moment of sampling. Each element is a virtual memory address inside ClickHouse server process.

column.name: size
column.Comment: For trace types Memory, MemorySample, MemoryAllocatedWithoutCheck or MemoryPeak is the amount of memory allocated, for other trace types is 0.

column.name: ptr
column.Comment: The address of the allocated chunk.

column.name: memory_context
column.Comment: Memory Tracker context (only for Memory/MemoryPeak): `Unknown` context is not defined for this trace_type. `Global` represents server context. `User` represents user/merge context. `Process` represents process (i.e. query) context. `Thread` represents thread (thread of particular process) context. `Max` this is a special value means that memory tracker is not blocked (for blocked_context column).

column.name: memory_blocked_context
column.Comment: Context for which memory tracker is blocked (for ClickHouse developers only): `Unknown` context is not defined for this trace_type. `Global` represents server context. `User` represents user/merge context. `Process` represents process (i.e. query) context. `Thread` represents thread (thread of particular process) context. `Max` this is a special value means that memory tracker is not blocked (for blocked_context column).

column.name: event
column.Comment: For trace type ProfileEvent is the name of updated profile event, for other trace types is an empty string.

column.name: increment
column.Comment: For trace type ProfileEvent is the amount of increment of profile event, for other trace types is 0.

column.name: symbols
column.Comment: If the symbolization is enabled, contains demangled symbol names, corresponding to the `trace`.

column.name: lines
column.Comment: If the symbolization is enabled, contains strings with file names with line numbers, corresponding to the `trace`.

column.name: function_id
column.Comment: For trace type Instrumentation, ID assigned to the function in xray_instr_map section of elf-binary.

column.name: function_name
column.Comment: For trace type Instrumentation, name of the instrumented function.

column.name: handler
column.Comment: For trace type Instrumentation, handler of the instrumented function.

column.name: entry_type
column.Comment: For trace type Instrumentation, entry type of the instrumented function.

column.name: duration_nanoseconds
column.Comment: For trace type Instrumentation, time the function was running for in nanoseconds.

column.name: name
column.Comment: UDF name.

column.name: load_status
column.Comment: Loading status. Possible values: Success — UDF loaded and ready to use, Failed — UDF failed to load (see field 'loading_error_message' for details).

column.name: loading_error_message
column.Comment: Detailed error message when loading failed. Empty if loaded successfully.

column.name: last_successful_update_time
column.Comment: Timestamp of the last successful update. NULL if never succeeded.

column.name: loading_duration_ms
column.Comment: Time spent loading the UDF, in milliseconds.

column.name: type
column.Comment: UDF type: 'executable' (single process) or 'executable_pool' (process pool).

column.name: command
column.Comment: Script or command to execute for this UDF.

column.name: format
column.Comment: Data format for I/O (e.g., 'TabSeparated', 'JSONEachRow').

column.name: return_type
column.Comment: Function return type (e.g., 'String', 'UInt64').

column.name: return_name
column.Comment: Optional return value identifier. Empty if not configured.

column.name: argument_types
column.Comment: Array of argument types (e.g., ['String', 'UInt64']).

column.name: argument_names
column.Comment: Array of argument names. Empty strings for unnamed arguments.

column.name: max_command_execution_time
column.Comment: Maximum seconds to process a data block. Only for 'executable_pool' type.

column.name: command_termination_timeout
column.Comment: Seconds before sending SIGTERM to command process.

column.name: command_read_timeout
column.Comment: Milliseconds for reading from command stdout.

column.name: command_write_timeout
column.Comment: Milliseconds for writing to command stdin.

column.name: pool_size
column.Comment: Number of command process instances. Only for 'executable_pool' type.

column.name: send_chunk_header
column.Comment: Whether to send row count before each data chunk (boolean).

column.name: execute_direct
column.Comment: Whether to execute command directly (1) or via /bin/bash (0).

column.name: lifetime
column.Comment: Reload interval in seconds. 0 means reload is disabled.

column.name: deterministic
column.Comment: Whether function returns the same result for the same arguments (boolean).

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of the directory.

column.name: type
column.Comment: The type of the access storage e.g. users.xml or replicated or memory etc.

column.name: params
column.Comment: JSON with the parameters of the access storage.

column.name: precedence
column.Comment: The order in which this directory is declared in the config. The same order is used when ClickHouse tries to find a user or role.

column.name: user
column.Comment: User name.

column.name: memory_usage
column.Comment: Sum of RAM used by all processes of the user. It might not include some types of dedicated memory. See the max_memory_usage setting.

column.name: peak_memory_usage
column.Comment: The peak of memory usage of the user. It can be reset when no queries are run for the user.

column.name: ProfileEvents
column.Comment: Summary of ProfileEvents that measure different metrics for the user. The description of them could be found in the table system.events

column.name: name
column.Comment: User name.

column.name: id
column.Comment: User ID.

column.name: storage
column.Comment: Path to the storage of users. Configured in the access_control_path parameter.

column.name: auth_type
column.Comment: Shows the authentication types. There are multiple ways of user identification: with no password, with plain text password, with SHA256-encoded password, with double SHA-1-encoded password or with bcrypt-encoded password.

column.name: auth_params
column.Comment: Authentication parameters in the JSON format depending on the auth_type.

column.name: valid_until
column.Comment: The expiration date and time for user credentials.

column.name: host_ip
column.Comment: IP addresses of hosts that are allowed to connect to the ClickHouse server.

column.name: host_names
column.Comment: Names of hosts that are allowed to connect to the ClickHouse server.

column.name: host_names_regexp
column.Comment: Regular expression for host names that are allowed to connect to the ClickHouse server.

column.name: host_names_like
column.Comment: Names of hosts that are allowed to connect to the ClickHouse server, set using the LIKE predicate.

column.name: default_roles_all
column.Comment: Shows that all granted roles set for user by default.

column.name: default_roles_list
column.Comment: List of granted roles provided by default.

column.name: default_roles_except
column.Comment: All the granted roles set as default excepting of the listed ones.

column.name: grantees_any
column.Comment: The flag that indicates whether a user with any grant option can grant it to anyone.

column.name: grantees_list
column.Comment: The list of users or roles to which this user is allowed to grant options to.

column.name: grantees_except
column.Comment: The list of users or roles to which this user is forbidden from grant options to.

column.name: default_database
column.Comment: The name of the default database for this user.

column.name: database
column.Comment: The name of the database the table is in.

column.name: view
column.Comment: Table name.

column.name: uuid
column.Comment: Table uuid (Atomic database).

column.name: status
column.Comment: Current state of the refresh.

column.name: last_success_time
column.Comment: Time when the latest successful refresh started. NULL if no successful refreshes happened since server startup or table creation.

column.name: last_success_duration_ms
column.Comment: How long the latest refresh took.

column.name: last_refresh_time
column.Comment: Time when the latest refresh attempt finished (if known) or started (if unknown or still running). NULL if no refresh attempts happened since server startup or table creation.

column.name: last_refresh_replica
column.Comment: If coordination is enabled, name of the replica that made the current (if running) or previous (if not running) refresh attempt.

column.name: next_refresh_time
column.Comment: Time at which the next refresh is scheduled to start, if status = Scheduled.

column.name: exception
column.Comment: Error message from previous attempt if it failed.

column.name: retry
column.Comment: How many failed attempts there were so far, for the current refresh. Not available if status is `RunningOnAnotherReplica`.

column.name: progress
column.Comment: Progress of the current running or most recently completed refresh at the given replica, between 0 and 1. NULL if status is `RunningOnAnotherReplica` or the refresh is not running.

column.name: read_rows
column.Comment: Number of rows read by the current running or most recently completed refresh at the given replica. NULL if status is `RunningOnAnotherReplica`.

column.name: read_bytes
column.Comment: Number of bytes read by the current running or most recently completed refresh at the given replica. NULL if status is `RunningOnAnotherReplica`

column.name: total_rows
column.Comment: Estimated total number of rows that need to be read by the current running or most recently completed refresh at the given replica. NULL if status is `RunningOnAnotherReplica`

column.name: written_rows
column.Comment: Number of rows written by the current running or most recently completed refresh at the given replica. NULL if status is `RunningOnAnotherReplica`

column.name: written_bytes
column.Comment: Number of bytes written by the current running or most recently completed refresh at the given replica. NULL if status is `RunningOnAnotherReplica`

column.name: message
column.Comment: A warning message issued by ClickHouse server.

column.name: message_format_string
column.Comment: A format string that was used to format the message.

column.name: name
column.Comment: The name of the workload.

column.name: parent
column.Comment: The name of the parent workload.

column.name: create_query
column.Comment: CREATE query of the workload.

column.name: zero
column.Comment: dummy

column.name: zero
column.Comment: dummy
